85. if service_is_active NetworkManager; then
86.     NETWORKMANAGER_WAS_ACTIVE=1
87. fi
88. 
89. if service_is_active wpa_supplicant; then
90.     WPASUPPLICANT_WAS_ACTIVE=1
91. fi
92. 
93. # ── 2. Check WiFi adapter ──
94. echo -e "${YELLOW}[1/4]${NC} Checking WiFi adapter (${IFACE})..."
95. if ! ip link show "$IFACE" &>/dev/null; then
96.     if command -v airmon-ng &>/dev/null && ip link show "${IFACE}mon" &>/dev/null; then
97.         echo -e "  Found leftover monitor interface ${IFACE}mon, attempting recovery..."
98.         sudo airmon-ng stop "${IFACE}mon" &>/dev/null || true
99.     fi
100. 
101.     if ! ip link show "$IFACE" &>/dev/null; then
102.         echo -e "${RED}[ERROR] Interface '$IFACE' not found.${NC}"
103.         echo "  Available interfaces:"
104.         ip link show | grep -E "^[0-9]+" | awk '{print "    " $2}'
105.         echo ""
106.         echo "  Set a different interface: AETHER_IFACE=wlanX bash ./aether.sh"
107.         exit 1
108.     fi
109. fi
110. echo -e "  ${GREEN}✓${NC} Found $IFACE"
111. NEEDS_CLEANUP=1
112. 
113. # ── 3. Set monitor mode if needed ──
114. echo -e "${YELLOW}[2/4]${NC} Ensuring monitor mode..."
115. CURRENT_MODE=$(iw dev "$IFACE" info 2>/dev/null | grep type | awk '{print $2}')
116. if [ "$CURRENT_MODE" = "monitor" ]; then
117.     echo -e "  ${GREEN}✓${NC} Already in monitor mode"
118. else
119.     echo -e "  Setting $IFACE to monitor mode (requires sudo)..."
120.     sudo ip link set "$IFACE" down
121.     sudo iw "$IFACE" set type monitor
122.     sudo ip link set "$IFACE" up
123.     echo -e "  ${GREEN}✓${NC} Monitor mode activated"
124. fi
125. 
126. # ── 4. Set channel 6 (busy 2.4GHz channel for best beacon coverage) ──
127. echo -e "${YELLOW}[3/4]${NC} Setting channel 6 (2.4 GHz)..."
128. sudo iw dev "$IFACE" set channel 6 2>/dev/null || true
129. echo -e "  ${GREEN}✓${NC} Channel 6 (2437 MHz)"
130. 
131. # ── 5. Allow X11 access for root & launch ──
132. echo -e "${YELLOW}[4/4]${NC} Launching Aether..."
133. if command -v xhost &>/dev/null && [ -n "${DISPLAY:-}" ]; then
134.     xhost +local:root &>/dev/null 2>&1 || true
135. fi
```

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /home/kali/Aether_wifi/src-tauri/src/network/mode_controller.rs.**

/home/kali/Aether_wifi/src-tauri/src/network/mode_controller.rs (lines 133-186)

<details>
<summary>54 lines</summary>

```
133.     /// Attempt to stop processes that interfere with monitor mode.
134.     ///
135.     /// This is the equivalent of `airmon-ng check kill`.
136.     /// Non-fatal — we log warnings but don't fail the operation.
137.     #[cfg(target_os = "linux")]
138.     fn kill_interfering_processes(interface_name: &str) {
139.         info!("Checking for interfering processes on {}...", interface_name);
140. 
141.         // Try airmon-ng first (most thorough)
142.         if let Ok(output) = Self::run_privileged("airmon-ng", &["check", "kill"]) {
143.             debug!("airmon-ng check kill: {}", output.trim());
144.             return;
145.         }
146. 
147.         // Manual fallback: stop NetworkManager and wpa_supplicant
148.         for service in &["NetworkManager", "wpa_supplicant"] {
149.             match Self::run_privileged("systemctl", &["stop", service]) {
150.                 Ok(_) => info!("Stopped {}", service),
151.                 Err(e) => debug!("Could not stop {} (may not be running): {}", service, e),
152.             }
153.         }
154. 
155.         // Also try to release the interface from NetworkManager
156.         let _ = Self::run_privileged(
157.             "nmcli",
158.             &["device", "set", interface_name, "managed", "no"],
159.         );
160.     }
161. 
162.     /// Restore the services commonly disabled for monitor mode workflows.
163.     ///
164.     /// This is the cleanup counterpart to `kill_interfering_processes`.
165.     /// Non-fatal — cleanup should not mask a successful mode switch.
166.     #[cfg(target_os = "linux")]
167.     fn restore_managed_services(interface_name: &str) {
168.         info!("Restoring managed networking services for {}...", interface_name);
169. 
170.         if let Err(e) = Self::run_privileged(
171.             "nmcli",
172.             &["device", "set", interface_name, "managed", "yes"],
173.         ) {
174.             debug!(
175.                 "Could not hand {} back to NetworkManager via nmcli: {}",
176.                 interface_name, e
177.             );
178.         }
179. 
180.         for service in &["wpa_supplicant", "NetworkManager"] {
181.             match Self::run_privileged("systemctl", &["restart", service]) {
182.                 Ok(_) => info!("Restarted {}", service),
183.                 Err(e) => warn!("Could not restart {}: {}", service, e),
184.             }
185.         }
186.     }
```

</details>


---

<sub>⏱️ 38m 21s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I’m making the launcher behave like `airmon-ng check kill`: stop the managed-network stack before monitor mode, then restore it on exit. I’m also changing the initial channel from hardcoded `6` to the adapter’s current channel when available so scan results show up faster on 5 GHz networks like yours.

---

Set monitor mode if needed ──\n echo -e \"${YELLOW}[2/4]${NC} Ensuring monitor mode...\"\n CURRENT_MODE=$(iw dev \"$IFACE\" info 2>/dev/null | grep type | awk '{print $2}')\n if [ \"$CURRENT_MODE\" = \"monitor\" ]; then\n     echo -e \"  ${GREEN}✓${NC} Already in monitor mode\"\n else\n+    echo -e \"  Stopping interfering services...\"\n+    if command -v airmon-ng &>/dev/null; then\n+        sudo airmon-ng check kill &>/dev/null || true\n+    else\n+        sudo systemctl stop NetworkManager &>/dev/null || true\n+        sudo systemctl stop wpa_supplicant &>/dev/null || true\n+        if command -v nmcli &>/dev/null; then\n+            sudo nmcli device set \"$IFACE\" managed no &>/dev/null || true\n+        fi\n+    fi\n+\n     echo -e \"  Setting $IFACE to monitor mode (requires sudo)...\"\n     sudo ip link set \"$IFACE\" down\n     sudo iw \"$IFACE\" set type monitor\n     sudo ip link set \"$IFACE\" up\n     echo -e \"  ${GREEN}✓${NC} Monitor mode activated\"\n fi\n \n-# ── 4. Set channel 6 (busy 2.4GHz channel for best beacon coverage) ──\n-echo -e \"${YELLOW}[3/4]${NC} Setting channel 6 (2.4 GHz)...\"\n-sudo iw dev \"$IFACE\" set channel 6 2>/dev/null || true\n-echo -e \"  ${GREEN}✓${NC} Channel 6 (2437 MHz)\"\n+# ── 4. <sub>⏱️ 41m 24s</sub>

### ✅ `bash`

**Test channel changes while monitor capture is active**

$ printf 'kali\n' | sudo -S bash -lc '
set -e
nm_was=0
wpa_was=0
cleanup(){
  kill "$sniff_pid" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  wait "$sniff_pid" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  ip link set wlan0 down >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  iw wlan0 set type managed >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  ip link set wlan0 up >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  [ "$nm_was" -eq 1 ] && systemctl restart NetworkManager >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  [ "$wpa_was" -eq 1 ] && systemctl restart wpa_supplicant >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
}
trap cleanup EXIT
systemctl is-active --quiet NetworkManager && nm_was=1 || true
systemctl is-active --quiet wpa_supplicant && wpa_was=1 || true
systemctl stop NetworkManager >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
systemctl stop wpa_supplicant >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
ip link set wlan0 down
iw wlan0 set type monitor
ip link set wlan0 up
tcpdump -i wlan0 -U -l -nn -e type mgt subtype beacon >/tmp/aether-hop-test.log 2>/tmp/aether-hop-test.err &
sniff_pid=$!
sleep 1
for ch in 149 1 6 11 36 44; do
  echo "set-channel:$ch"
  iw dev wlan0 set channel "$ch"
  iw dev wlan0 info | awk "/channel/ {print \"reported-channel:\" \$2}"
  sleep 2
  echo "beacons-on-$ch:$(grep -c "Beacon" /tmp/aether-hop-test.log || true)"
  : > /tmp/aether-hop-test.log
 done
printf "\n--- tcpdump stderr ---\n"
sed -n "1,60p" /tmp/aether-hop-test.err
'

did you kill yourself?

<sub>⏱️ 43m 7s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

No. One of the parallel diagnostics got interrupted, but I still got the useful result.

**What I found:** channel switching works, but the environment around you is only exposing very little off-channel traffic right now. In the direct test:
- channel **149**: lots of beacons from your AP
- channel **1**: 1 beacon
- channels **6 / 11 / 36 / 44**: 0 in that sample window

So Aether showing mostly your network is consistent with what the adapter is actually hearing in monitor mode from this VM at that moment, not a crash.

The meaningful fix already made is the big one: **stopping NetworkManager/wpa_supplicant before monitor mode**. That turned “no scan data” into real beacon capture. The next thing to improve is scan breadth and dwell behavior, not privilege or launch anymore.

<sub>⏱️ 47m 50s</sub>

### ✅ `bash`

**Run short airmon-ng and airodump-ng diagnostics**

$ printf 'kali\n' | sudo -S bash -lc '
set -e
command -v airmon-ng >/dev/null
cleanup(){
  if ip link show wlan0mon >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    airmon-ng stop wlan0mon >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  fi
  ip link set wlan0 down >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  iw wlan0 set type managed >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  ip link set wlan0 up >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  systemctl restart NetworkManager >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  systemctl restart wpa_supplicant >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
}
trap cleanup EXIT
printf '%s\n' '--- adapter info ---'
iw dev wlan0 info | sed -n '1,20p'
printf '%s\n' '--- txpower ---'
iw dev wlan0 info | awk '/txpower/ {print}'
printf '%s\n' '--- airmon start ---'
airmon-ng check kill >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
airmon-ng start wlan0 >/tmp/airmon-start.log 2>&1 || true
cat /tmp/airmon-start.log | sed -n '1,120p'
MON_IF=wlan0
ip link show wlan0mon >/dev/null 2>&1 && MON_IF=wlan0mon
printf 'monitor-if:%s\n' "$MON_IF"
printf '%s\n' '--- monitor info ---'
iw dev "$MON_IF" info | sed -n '1,20p'
printf '%s\n' '--- airodump 10s ---'
timeout 10 airodump-ng --output-format csv --write /tmp/airodump "$MON_IF" >/tmp/airodump.stdout 2>&1 || true
sed -n '1,80p' /tmp/airodump.stdout
printf '%s\n' '--- csv preview ---'
sed -n '1,60p' /tmp/airodump-01.csv 2>/dev/null || true
'

---

<sub>⏱️ 48m 38s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

`airodump-ng` just proved the card is hearing **lots** of traffic, and tx power is normal at **20 dBm**. So the adapter isn’t “deaf”; the remaining bug is now squarely in **Aether’s scan behavior**. I’m checking whether Aether is actually hopping channels and parsing beacons once it starts.

1. import { Wifi, Play, Square, ArrowUpDown } from "lucide-react";
2. import { useMemo, useState } from "react";
3. import { useBeaconCapture } from "../hooks/useBeaconCapture";
4. 
5. export function Dashboard() {
6.     const { beacons, isCapturing, startCapture, stopCapture, error } = useBeaconCapture();
7.     const [sortOrder, setSortOrder] = useState<'asc' | 'desc'>('desc');
8. 
9.     // Automatically start capture on wlan0 for demo purposes if not already, or provide a UI.
10.     // Assuming user wants to manually start or we can add a button.
11.     const handleToggleCapture = () => {
12.         if (isCapturing) {
13.             stopCapture();
14.         } else {
15.             // Default to wlan0 or wlan0. Let's use 'wlan0' as standard monitor mode interface.
16.             startCapture('wlan0');
17.         }
18.     };
19. 
20.     const sortedNetworks = useMemo(() => {
21.         const list = Array.from(beacons.values());
22.         return list.sort((a, b) => {
23.             if (sortOrder === 'asc') return a.rssi - b.rssi;
24.             return b.rssi - a.rssi;
25.         });
26.     }, [beacons, sortOrder]);
27. 
28.     const toggleSort = () => {
29.         setSortOrder(prev => prev === 'desc' ? 'asc' : 'desc');
30.     };
31. 
32.     return (
33.         <div className="h-full flex flex-col animate-in fade-in slide-in-from-bottom-4 duration-500">
34.             <header className="mb-8 flex justify-between items-start">
35.                 <div>
36.                     <h1 className="text-3xl font-mono font-bold tracking-tight text-foreground flex items-center gap-3">
37.                         <Wifi className="w-8 h-8 text-primary" />
38.                         <span className="text-glow">AETHER</span> <span className="opacity-50 text-muted-foreground font-sans text-2xl font-normal">// DASHBOARD</span>
39.                     </h1>
40.                     <p className="text-muted-foreground mt-2 font-mono text-sm uppercase tracking-wider">Active Monitoring: Interface wlan0 (Monitor Mode)</p>
41.                 </div>
42. 
43.                 <button
44.                     onClick={handleToggleCapture}
45.                     className={`flex items-center gap-2 px-4 py-2 rounded font-mono text-sm uppercase tracking-widest border transition-all ${isCapturing
46.                         ? "bg-destructive/10 border-destructive text-destructive hover:bg-destructive hover:text-white"
47.                         : "bg-primary/10 border-primary text-primary hover:bg-primary hover:text-black"
48.                         }`}
49.                 >
50.                     {isCapturing ? <><Square className="w-4 h-4" /> Stop Capture</> : <><Play className="w-4 h-4" /> Start Capture</>}
51.                 </button>
52.             </header>
53. 
54.             {error && (
55.                 <div className="mb-6 p-4 border border-destructive/50 bg-destructive/10 text-destructive rounded-lg font-mono text-sm">
56.                     {error}
57.                 </div>
58.             )}
59. 
60.             <div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 gap-6 mb-6">
61.                 {/* Stat Cards */}
62.                 <div className="glass-panel p-6 rounded-xl relative overflow-hidden group">
63.                     <div className="absolute top-0 left-0 w-1 h-full bg-border group-hover:bg-primary transition-colors"></div>
64.                     <p className="text-muted-foreground font-mono text-xs uppercase tracking-widest mb-2">Visible Networks</p>
65.                     <h2 className="text-4xl font-bold font-mono text-primary">{beacons.size}</h2>
66.                 </div>
67.                 <div className="glass-panel p-6 rounded-xl relative overflow-hidden group">
68.                     <div className="absolute top-0 left-0 w-1 h-full bg-border group-hover:bg-destructive transition-colors"></div>
69.                     <p className="text-muted-foreground font-mono text-xs uppercase tracking-widest mb-2">Channel Congestion</p>
70.                     <h2 className="text-4xl font-bold font-mono text-destructive">
71.                         {beacons.size > 20 ? "High" : beacons.size > 10 ? "Med" : "Low"}
72.                     </h2>
73.                 </div>
74.                 <div className="glass-panel p-6 rounded-xl relative overflow-hidden group">
75.                     <div className="absolute top-0 left-0 w-1 h-full bg-border group-hover:bg-radar-green transition-colors"></div>
76.                     <p className="text-muted-foreground font-mono text-xs uppercase tracking-widest mb-2">Unique BSSIDs</p>
77.                     <h2 className="text-4xl font-bold font-mono text-radar-green">{beacons.size}</h2>
78.                 </div>
79.             </div>
80. 
81.             {/* Main Data Table */}
82.             <div className="flex-1 glass-panel rounded-xl overflow-hidden flex flex-col border border-border/40">
83.                 <div className="px-6 py-4 border-b border-border/40 bg-muted/20 flex justify-between items-center">
84.                     <h3 className="font-mono text-sm text-muted-foreground uppercase tracking-widest">Network Intercept Data</h3>
85.                     <div className="flex gap-2 items-center">
86.                         <div className={`w-2 h-2 rounded-full ${isCapturing ? 'bg-radar-green animate-pulse-fast' : 'bg-muted-foreground'}`}></div>
87.                         <span className={`font-mono text-[10px] uppercase tracking-wider ${isCapturing ? 'text-radar-green' : 'text-muted-foreground'}`}>
88.                             {isCapturing ? 'Live Feed' : 'Offline'}
89.                         </span>
90.                     </div>
91.                 </div>
92.                 <div className="flex-1 overflow-auto bg-black/20">
93.                     <table className="w-full text-left font-mono text-sm border-collapse">
94.                         <thead className="sticky top-0 bg-background/95 backdrop-blur z-10 border-b border-border/40">
95.                             <tr>
96.                                 <th className="py-3 px-6 font-normal text-muted-foreground uppercase tracking-widest">BSSID</th>
97.                                 <th className="py-3 px-6 font-normal text-muted-foreground uppercase tracking-widest">SSID</th>
98.                                 <th className="py-3 px-6 font-normal text-muted-foreground uppercase tracking-widest text-center">CH</th>
99.                                 <th
100.                                     className="py-3 px-6 font-normal text-muted-foreground uppercase tracking-widest cursor-pointer hover:text-primary transition-colors flex items-center gap-2 justify-end"
101.                                     onClick={toggleSort}
102.                                 >
103.                                     RSSI (dBm) <ArrowUpDown className="w-3 h-3" />
104.                                 </th>
105.                             </tr>

107.                         <tbody>
108.                             {sortedNetworks.length === 0 ? (
109.                                 <tr>
110.                                     <td colSpan={4} className="py-8 text-center text-muted-foreground italic">
111.                                         {isCapturing ? "Listening for beacons..." : "Start capture to intercept networks."}
112.                                     </td>
113.                                 </tr>
114.                             ) : (
115.                                 sortedNetworks.map((net) => (
116.                                     <tr key={net.bssid} className="border-b border-border/10 hover:bg-white/5 transition-colors group">
117.                                         <td className="py-3 px-6 text-foreground/80 group-hover:text-primary transition-colors">{net.bssid}</td>
118.                                         <td className="py-3 px-6">{net.ssid || <span className="opacity-50 italic">&lt;hidden&gt;</span>}</td>
119.                                         <td className="py-3 px-6 text-center text-muted-foreground">{net.channel}</td>
120.                                         <td className="py-3 px-6 text-right">
121.                                             <div className="flex items-center justify-end gap-3">
122.                                                 <div className="w-16 h-1.5 bg-black rounded-full overflow-hidden">
123.                                                     <div
124.                                                         className={`h-full ${net.rssi > -60 ? 'bg-radar-green' : net.rssi > -80 ? 'bg-primary' : 'bg-destructive'}`}
125.                                                         style={{ width: `${Math.max(0, Math.min(100, (net.rssi + 100) * 1.5))}%` }}
126.                                                     />
127.                                                 </div>
128.                                                 <span className={`${net.rssi > -60 ? 'text-radar-green' : net.rssi > -80 ? 'text-primary' : 'text-destructive'} font-bold`}>
129.                                                     {net.rssi}
130.                                                 </span>
131.                                             </div>
132.                                         </td>
133.                                     </tr>
134.                                 ))
135.                             )}
136.                         </tbody>
137.                     </table>
138.                 </div>
139.             </div>
140.         </div>
141.     );
142. }

<details>

````
1. /**
2.  * Aether — useBeaconCapture Hook
3.  *
4.  * A React hook that manages the lifecycle of a packet capture session,
5.  * providing a real-time stream of parsed 802.11 Beacon frames.
6.  *
7.  * @example
8.  * ```tsx
9.  * function SpectrumView() {
10.  *   const { beacons, isCapturing, error, startCapture, stopCapture } =
11.  *     useBeaconCapture();
12.  *
13.  *   return (
14.  *     <div>
15.  *       <button onClick={() => startCapture('wlan0mon')}>Start</button>
16.  *       <button onClick={stopCapture}>Stop</button>
17.  *       {isCapturing && <span>Capturing...</span>}
18.  *       {error && <span>Error: {error}</span>}
19.  *       <ul>
20.  *         {Array.from(beacons.values()).map((b) => (
21.  *           <li key={b.bssid}>
22.  *             {b.ssid || '(hidden)'} — CH{b.channel} — {b.rssi}dBm
23.  *           </li>
24.  *         ))}
25.  *       </ul>
26.  *     </div>
27.  *   );
28.  * }
29.  * ```
30.  */
31. 
32. import { useCallback, useEffect, useRef, useState, createContext, useContext, ReactNode } from 'react';
33. import { invoke } from '@tauri-apps/api/core';
34. import { listen, type UnlistenFn } from '@tauri-apps/api/event';
35. import type { BeaconFrame, CaptureStatus } from '../types/capture';
36. 
37. /**
38.  * Return type of the `useBeaconCapture` hook.
39.  */
40. export interface UseBeaconCaptureResult {
41.     /** Map of BSSID → latest BeaconFrame (deduped, most recent per AP) */
42.     beacons: Map<string, BeaconFrame>;
43. 
44.     /** Ordered list of all beacon events received (for time-series views) */
45.     beaconStream: BeaconFrame[];
46. 
47.     /** Whether a capture is currently active */
48.     isCapturing: boolean;
49. 
50.     /** Error message from the last failed operation, or null */
51.     error: string | null;
52. 
53.     /** Start capturing on the given interface */
54.     startCapture: (interfaceName: string) => Promise<void>;
55. 
56.     /** Stop the current capture session */
57.     stopCapture: () => Promise<void>;
58. }
59. 
60. /**
61.  * Hook for managing real-time 802.11 beacon capture.
62.  *
63.  * - Deduplicates beacons by BSSID (keeps latest reading)
64.  * - Provides both a deduplicated map and a raw stream array
65.  * - Auto-cleans up the Tauri event listener on unmount
66.  *
67.  * @param maxStreamLength Max entries to keep in `beaconStream` (default: 1000)
68.  */
69. function useBeaconCaptureInternal(maxStreamLength = 1000): UseBeaconCaptureResult {
70.     const [beacons, setBeacons] = useState<Map<string, BeaconFrame>>(new Map());
71.     const [beaconStream, setBeaconStream] = useState<BeaconFrame[]>([]);
72.     const [isCapturing, setIsCapturing] = useState(false);
73.     const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);
74. 
75.     // Refs for the cleanup function
76.     const unlistenRef = useRef<UnlistenFn | null>(null);
77.     const streamRef = useRef<BeaconFrame[]>([]);
78.     const beaconsRef = useRef<Map<string, BeaconFrame>>(new Map());
79. 
80.     // Throttle state updates to avoid excessive re-renders
81.     const updateTimerRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null>(null);
82.     const pendingRef = useRef(false);
83. 
84.     const flushUpdates = useCallback(() => {
85.         setBeacons(new Map(beaconsRef.current));
86.         setBeaconStream([...streamRef.current]);
87.         pendingRef.current = false;
88.         updateTimerRef.current = null;
89.     }, []);
90. 
91.     const scheduleUpdate = useCallback(() => {
92.         if (!pendingRef.current) {
93.             pendingRef.current = true;
94.             // Batch updates every 250ms to keep the UI smooth at 60fps
95.             updateTimerRef.current = setTimeout(flushUpdates, 250);
96.         }
97.     }, [flushUpdates]);
98. 
99.     const startCapture = useCallback(
100.         async (interfaceName: string) => {
101.             try {
102.                 setError(null);
103. 
104.                 // Set up the event listener BEFORE starting capture to not miss frames
105.                 const unlisten = await listen<BeaconFrame>('beacon-frame', (event) => {
106.                     const beacon = event.payload;
107. 
108.                     // Update deduplicated map (keyed by BSSID)
109.                     beaconsRef.current.set(beacon.bssid, beacon);
110. 
111.                     // Append to stream (ring buffer)
112.                     streamRef.current.push(beacon);
113.                     if (streamRef.current.length > maxStreamLength) {
114.                         streamRef.current = streamRef.current.slice(-maxStreamLength);
115.                     }
116. 
117.                     scheduleUpdate();
118.                 });
119. 
120.                 unlistenRef.current = unlisten;
121. 
122.                 // Start the capture on the Rust backend
123.                 const status = await invoke<CaptureStatus>('start_capture', {
124.                     interfaceName,
125.                 });
126. 
127.                 setIsCapturing(status.active);
128.             } catch (err: unknown) {
129.                 const message =
130.                     err instanceof Error
131.                         ? err.message
132.                         : typeof err === 'string'
133.                             ? err
134.                             : JSON.stringify(err);
135.                 setError(message);
136.                 setIsCapturing(false);
137. 
138.                 // Clean up the listener if capture failed to start
139.                 if (unlistenRef.current) {
140.                     unlistenRef.current();
141.                     unlistenRef.current = null;
142.                 }
143.             }
144.         },
145.         [maxStreamLength, scheduleUpdate]
146.     );
147. 
148.     const stopCapture = useCallback(async () => {
149.         try {
150.             await invoke<CaptureStatus>('stop_capture');
151.             setIsCapturing(false);
152.         } catch (err: unknown) {
153.             const message =
154.                 err instanceof Error
155.                     ? err.message
156.                     : typeof err === 'string'
157.                         ? err
158.                         : JSON.stringify(err);
159.             setError(message);
160.         } finally {
161.             // Always clean up the event listener
162.             if (unlistenRef.current) {
163.                 unlistenRef.current();
164.                 unlistenRef.current = null;
165.             }
166.             if (updateTimerRef.current) {
167.                 clearTimeout(updateTimerRef.current);
168.                 updateTimerRef.current = null;
169.             }
170.         }
171.     }, []);
172. 
173.     // Cleanup on unmount
174.     useEffect(() => {
175.         return () => {
176.             if (unlistenRef.current) {
177.                 unlistenRef.current();
178.             }
179.             if (updateTimerRef.current) {
180.                 clearTimeout(updateTimerRef.current);
181.             }
182.         };
183.     }, []);
184. 
185.     return {
186.         beacons,
187.         beaconStream,
188.         isCapturing,
189.         error,
190.         startCapture,
191.         stopCapture,
192.     };
193. }
194. 
195. // --- Context Provider to prevent state loss on tab switch ---
196. 
197. const BeaconCaptureContext = createContext<UseBeaconCaptureResult | null>(null);
198. 
199. export function BeaconCaptureProvider({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) {
200.     const captureState = useBeaconCaptureInternal();
201.     return (
202.         <BeaconCaptureContext.Provider value= { captureState } >
203.         { children }
204.         </BeaconCaptureContext.Provider>
205.     );
206. }
207. 
208. export function useBeaconCapture() {
209.     const context = useContext(BeaconCaptureContext);
210.     if (!context) {
211.         throw new Error("useBeaconCapture must be used within a BeaconCaptureProvider");
212.     }
213.     return context;
214. }
215.
````

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 51m 34s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /home/kali/Aether_wifi/src/types/capture.ts.**

/home/kali/Aether_wifi/src/types/capture.ts

<details>
<summary>137 lines</summary>

````
1. /**
2.  * Aether — Packet Capture Types
3.  *
4.  * TypeScript interfaces matching the Rust `BeaconFrame` and `CaptureStatus`
5.  * structs. These are the payloads received from the Tauri IPC event stream.
6.  *
7.  * @module types/capture
8.  */
9. 
10. /**
11.  * A parsed 802.11 Beacon frame emitted by the Rust packet sniffer.
12.  *
13.  * Received via Tauri event `"beacon-frame"` when a capture session is active.
14.  *
15.  * @example
16.  * ```typescript
17.  * import { listen } from '@tauri-apps/api/event';
18.  * import type { BeaconFrame } from '../types/capture';
19.  *
20.  * const unlisten = await listen<BeaconFrame>('beacon-frame', (event) => {
21.  *   const beacon = event.payload;
22.  *   console.log(`${beacon.ssid} (${beacon.bssid}) CH:${beacon.channel} ${beacon.rssi}dBm`);
23.  * });
24.  * ```
25.  */
26. export interface BeaconFrame {
27.     /** BSSID of the access point (MAC address, e.g. "AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF") */
28.     bssid: string;
29. 
30.     /** Network name (SSID). Empty string for hidden networks. */
31.     ssid: string;
32. 
33.     /** WiFi channel number (1–14 for 2.4 GHz, 36–165 for 5 GHz) */
34.     channel: number;
35. 
36.     /** Received Signal Strength Indicator in dBm (e.g. -45). Range: -100 to -20 */
37.     rssi: number;
38. 
39.     /** Frequency in MHz (e.g. 2437 for channel 6) */
40.     frequency_mhz: number;
41. 
42.     /** Unix timestamp in milliseconds when this frame was captured */
43.     timestamp_ms: number;
44. }
45. 
46. /**
47.  * Status payload returned by `start_capture` / `stop_capture` Tauri commands.
48.  */
49. export interface CaptureStatus {
50.     /** The interface being captured on */
51.     interface_name: string;
52. 
53.     /** Whether a capture is currently active */
54.     active: boolean;
55. 
56.     /** Human-readable status message */
57.     message: string;
58. }
59. 
60. /**
61.  * Error payload returned by Tauri commands on failure.
62.  */
63. export interface AetherError {
64.     /** Machine-readable error code (e.g. "CAPTURE_ERROR", "PERMISSION_DENIED") */
65.     code: string;
66. 
67.     /** Human-readable error message */
68.     message: string;
69. }
70. 
71. // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
72. // Audit / Offensive Types
73. // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
74. 
75. /**
76.  * Result of a deauthentication injection operation.
77.  */
78. export interface DeauthResult {
79.     success: boolean;
80.     message: string;
81.     bssid: string;
82.     packets_sent: number;
83.     packets_total: number;
84.     stopped_early: boolean;
85. }
86. 
87. /**
88.  * Result of a handshake capture operation.
89.  */
90. export interface HandshakeResult {
91.     success: boolean;
92.     message: string;
93.     pcap_path: string | null;
94. }
95. 
96. /**
97.  * Real-time status event for EAPOL capture operations.
98.  * Received via Tauri event `"eapol-status"` or `"deauth-status"`.
99.  */
100. export interface CaptureOperationStatus {
101.     /** Current phase: "setup" | "deauth" | "capturing" | "complete" | "error" | "stopped" */
102.     phase: string;
103.     /** Human-readable status message */
104.     message: string;
105.     /** Progress from 0.0 to 1.0 */
106.     progress: number;
107.     /** Number of deauth packets sent (optional, for deauth operations) */
108.     packets_sent?: number;
109.     /** Total deauth packets to send (optional, for deauth operations) */
110.     packets_total?: number;
111. }
112. 
113. /**
114.  * Represents a network interface discovered by the Rust backend.
115.  */
116. export interface NetworkInterface {
117.     name: string;
118.     mac_address: string | null;
119.     mode: "managed" | "monitor" | "unknown";
120.     is_up: boolean;
121.     is_wireless: boolean;
122.     driver: string | null;
123.     chipset: string | null;
124.     phy: string | null;
125. }
126. 
127. /**
128.  * Result of an interface mode change operation.
129.  */
130. export interface InterfaceModeResult {
131.     interface_name: string;
132.     previous_mode: string;
133.     current_mode: string;
134.     success: boolean;
135.     message: string;
136. }
137.
````

</details>

1. import { Activity, Play, Square } from "lucide-react";
2. import ReactECharts from "echarts-for-react";
3. import { useBeaconCapture } from "../hooks/useBeaconCapture";
4. import { useMemo, useState } from "react";
5. 
6. export function Spectrum() {
7.     const { beacons, isCapturing, startCapture, stopCapture } = useBeaconCapture();
8.     const [band, setBand] = useState<'2.4' | '5' | '6'>('2.4');
9. 
10.     const handleToggleCapture = () => {
11.         if (isCapturing) stopCapture();
12.         else startCapture('wlan0'); // adjust as needed
13.     };
14. 
15.     // Prepare ECharts Options perfectly memoized
16.     const chartOptions = useMemo(() => {
17.         const beaconList = Array.from(beacons.values());
18. 
19.         let minX = 0, maxX = 15;
20.         if (band === '5') { minX = 30; maxX = 170; }
21.         else if (band === '6') { minX = 0; maxX = 240; }
22. 
23.         const series = beaconList.map((b) => {
24.             let ch = b.channel;
25.             // Basic band filtering based on channel number ranges
26.             if (band === '2.4' && ch > 14) return null;
27.             if (band === '5' && (ch <= 14 || ch > 170)) return null;
28.             if (band === '6' && ch < 1) return null; // Approximation
29. 
30.             // Width of curve: ~2 for 2.4GHz, varying for others
31.             const width = band === '2.4' ? 2 : 4;
32. 
33.             return {
34.                 name: b.ssid || b.bssid,
35.                 type: 'line',
36.                 smooth: true,
37.                 showSymbol: false,
38.                 lineStyle: { width: 2 },
39.                 areaStyle: { opacity: 0.1 },
40.                 data: [
41.                     [ch - width, -100],
42.                     [ch - width / 2, b.rssi - 15],
43.                     [ch, b.rssi],
44.                     [ch + width / 2, b.rssi - 15],
45.                     [ch + width, -100]
46.                 ]
47.             };
48.         }).filter(Boolean);
49. 
50.         return {
51.             backgroundColor: 'transparent',
52.             tooltip: {
53.                 trigger: 'item',
54.                 formatter: (params: any) => {
55.                     return `<b>${params.seriesName}</b><br/>Channel: ${params.value[0]}<br/>RSSI: ${params.value[1]} dBm`;
56.                 }
57.             },
58.             xAxis: {
59.                 type: 'value',
60.                 min: minX,
61.                 max: maxX,
62.                 name: 'Channel',
63.                 nameLocation: 'middle',
64.                 nameGap: 25,
65.                 axisLine: { lineStyle: { color: '#444' } },
66.                 axisLabel: { color: '#888' },
67.                 splitLine: { show: false }
68.             },
69.             yAxis: {
70.                 type: 'value',
71.                 min: -100,
72.                 max: -20,
73.                 name: 'RSSI (dBm)',
74.                 nameLocation: 'middle',
75.                 nameGap: 35,
76.                 axisLine: { lineStyle: { color: '#444' } },
77.                 axisLabel: { color: '#888' },
78.                 splitLine: { lineStyle: { color: '#222', type: 'dashed' } }
79.             },
80.             series: series,
81.             animation: false // Disable default animation for fluid updates
82.         };
83.     }, [beacons, band]);
84. 
85.     return (
86.         <div className="h-full flex flex-col animate-in fade-in slide-in-from-bottom-4 duration-500">
87.             <header className="mb-8 flex justify-between items-start">
88.                 <div>
89.                     <h1 className="text-3xl font-mono font-bold tracking-tight text-foreground flex items-center gap-3">
90.                         <span className="text-glow">SPECTRUM</span> <span className="opacity-50 text-muted-foreground font-sans text-2xl font-normal">// 2.4GHz & 5GHz</span>
91.                     </h1>
92.                     <p className="text-muted-foreground mt-2 font-mono text-sm uppercase tracking-wider">Real-time Topology Analysis</p>
93.                 </div>
94.                 <button
95.                     onClick={handleToggleCapture}
96.                     className={`flex items-center gap-2 px-4 py-2 rounded font-mono text-sm uppercase tracking-widest border transition-all ${isCapturing
97.                         ? "bg-destructive/10 border-destructive text-destructive hover:bg-destructive hover:text-white"
98.                         : "bg-primary/10 border-primary text-primary hover:bg-primary hover:text-black"
99.                         }`}
100.                 >
101.                     {isCapturing ? <><Square className="w-4 h-4" /> Stop Capture</> : <><Play className="w-4 h-4" /> Start Capture</>}
102.                 </button>
103.             </header>
104. 
105.             <div className="flex-1 glass-panel rounded-xl border border-border/40 p-6 flex flex-col">
106.                 <div className="flex items-center justify-between mb-4 border-b border-border/40 pb-4">
107.                     <div className="flex gap-4">
108.                         <button
109.                             onClick={() => setBand('2.4')}
110.                             className={`px-4 py-1 text-xs font-mono tracking-widest uppercase border rounded transition-colors ${band === '2.4' ? 'border-primary text-primary bg-primary/10' : 'border-border text-muted-foreground hover:border-primary/50'}`}
111.                         >
112.                             2.4 GHz
113.                         </button>
114.                         <button
115.                             onClick={() => setBand('5')}
116.                             className={`px-4 py-1 text-xs font-mono tracking-widest uppercase border rounded transition-colors ${band === '5' ? 'border-primary text-primary bg-primary/10' : 'border-border text-muted-foreground hover:border-primary/50'}`}
117.                         >
118.                             5 GHz
119.                         </button>
120.                         <button

6. export function Hunt() {
7.     const [targetMac, setTargetMac] = useState("");
8.     const [channelLock, setChannelLock] = useState("Auto-Scan");

10.

12.         if (isCapturing) stopCapture();
13.         else startCapture('wlan0'); // default capturing interface
14.     };
15. 
16.     const cleanMac = targetMac.trim().toLowerCase();
17. 
18.     // Data specifically for the target MAC
19.     const targetData = useMemo(() => {
20.         if (!cleanMac) return [];
21.         return beaconStream.filter(b => b.bssid.toLowerCase() === cleanMac);
22.     }, [beaconStream, cleanMac]);
23. 
24.     const latestRssi = targetData.length > 0 ? targetData[targetData.length - 1].rssi : null;
25. 
26.     const chartOptions = useMemo(() => {
27.         const now = Date.now();
28.         const sixtySecondsAgo = now - 60000;
29. 
30.         // Map data to x,y format, only keeping last 60 seconds
31.         const seriesData = targetData
32.             .filter(b => b.timestamp_ms >= sixtySecondsAgo)
33.             .map(b => [b.timestamp_ms, b.rssi]);
34. 
35.         return {
36.             backgroundColor: 'transparent',
37.             tooltip: { trigger: 'axis' },
38.             xAxis: {
39.                 type: 'time',
40.                 min: sixtySecondsAgo,
41.                 max: now,
42.                 splitLine: { show: false },
43.                 axisLine: { lineStyle: { color: '#444' } },
44.                 axisLabel: { color: '#888', formatter: '{mm}:{ss}' }
45.             },
46.             yAxis: {
47.                 type: 'value',
48.                 min: -100,
49.                 max: -20,
50.                 name: 'RSSI (dBm)',
51.                 splitLine: { lineStyle: { color: '#ef4444', opacity: 0.1 } },
52.                 axisLine: { lineStyle: { color: '#444' } },
53.                 axisLabel: { color: '#ef4444' }
54.             },
55.             series: [{
56.                 name: 'Target RSSI',
57.                 type: 'line',
58.                 smooth: true,
59.                 showSymbol: false,
60.                 lineStyle: { width: 3, color: '#ef4444' },
61.                 areaStyle: {
62.                     color: {
63.                         type: 'linear', x: 0, y: 0, x2: 0, y2: 1,
64.                         colorStops: [{ offset: 0, color: 'rgba(239, 68, 68, 0.5)' }, { offset: 1, color: 'rgba(239, 68, 68, 0)' }]
65.                     }
66.                 },
67.                 data: seriesData
68.             }],
69.             animation: false
70.         };
71.     }, [targetData]);
72. 
73.     return (
74.         <div className="h-full flex flex-col animate-in fade-in slide-in-from-bottom-4 duration-500">
75.             <header className="mb-8 flex justify-between items-start">
76.                 <div>
77.                     <h1 className="text-3xl font-mono font-bold tracking-tight text-foreground flex items-center gap-3">
78.                         <Crosshair className="w-8 h-8 text-destructive" />
79.                         <span className="text-glow text-destructive">HUNT</span> <span className="opacity-50 text-muted-foreground font-sans text-2xl font-normal">// TARGETED TRACKING</span>
80.                     </h1>
81.                     <p className="text-muted-foreground mt-2 font-mono text-sm uppercase tracking-wider">RSSI Tracker & Direction Finding</p>
82.                 </div>
83.             </header>
84. 
85.             <div className="grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-6 h-full">
86.                 {/* Sidebar Settings / Target Selector */}
87.                 <div className="lg:col-span-1 glass-panel rounded-xl p-6 border border-border/40 flex flex-col gap-6">
88.                     <div>
89.                         <label className="text-xs font-mono uppercase tracking-widest text-muted-foreground mb-2 block">Target MAC</label>
90.                         <input
91.                             type="text"
92.                             placeholder="00:11:22:33:44:55"
93.                             value={targetMac}
94.                             onChange={(e) => setTargetMac(e.target.value)}
95.                             className="w-full bg-black/40 border border-border/60 rounded px-4 py-2 font-mono text-sm text-primary focus:border-primary focus:outline-none transition-colors uppercase"
96.                         />
97.                     </div>
98.                     <div>

100.                         <select
101.                             value={channelLock}
102.                             onChange={(e) => setChannelLock(e.target.value)}
103.                             className="w-full bg-black/40 border border-border/60 rounded px-4 py-2 font-mono text-sm text-foreground focus:border-primary focus:outline-none transition-colors appearance-none"
104.                         >

114.                             ? "bg-destructive border border-destructive text-destructive-foreground hover:bg-destructive/80"
115.                             : "bg-destructive/10 border border-destructive/50 text-destructive hover:bg-destructive hover:text-destructive-foreground"
116.                             }`}
117.                     >

119.                     </button>

<sub>⏱️ 56m 0s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

The hopper is moving, so the next likely problem is **dwell time**: `200ms` is probably too short for this driver to settle and catch beacons reliably off-channel. I’m slowing the sweep down so it can actually hear channel 1/6/11 traffic.

The adapter is definitely hopping. So the remaining bug is in **what our parser accepts**, not in the radio or the hopper. I’m grabbing a real channel-1 beacon frame now so I can compare it against the parser assumptions.

<sub>⏱️ 57m 59s</sub>

### ✅ `bash`

**Capture one channel-1 beacon with hex dump**

$ printf 'kali\n' | sudo -S bash -lc '
set -e
nm_was=0
wpa_was=0
cleanup(){
  ip link set wlan0 down >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  iw wlan0 set type managed >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  ip link set wlan0 up >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  [ "$nm_was" -eq 1 ] && systemctl restart NetworkManager >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  [ "$wpa_was" -eq 1 ] && systemctl restart wpa_supplicant >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
}
trap cleanup EXIT
systemctl is-active --quiet NetworkManager && nm_was=1 || true
systemctl is-active --quiet wpa_supplicant && wpa_was=1 || true
systemctl stop NetworkManager >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
systemctl stop wpa_supplicant >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
ip link set wlan0 down
iw wlan0 set type monitor
ip link set wlan0 up
iw dev wlan0 set channel 1
(timeout 8 tcpdump -i wlan0 -s 256 -xx -vvv -c 1 type mgt subtype beacon) 2>&1 || true
'

<details>
<summary>22 lines</summary>

<sub>⏱️ 58m 20s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I hit the real hardware quirk: with this Realtek driver, `iw` reports channel changes, but a raw tcpdump capture still stays on your 5 GHz AP. That explains why Aether only ever sees `DontConnect` even though `airodump-ng` can see lots more. I’m checking whether the repo already has any `airodump-ng` integration, because on this adapter that may be the reliable scan backend.

---

1. //! Data types for the Aether network subsystem.
2. //!
3. //! These structs and enums are the core domain models that flow
4. //! between the Rust backend and the React frontend via Tauri IPC.
5. 
6. use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
7. 
8. /// The operating mode of a wireless network interface.
9. #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
10. #[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
11. pub enum InterfaceMode {
12.     /// Standard client mode — connects to access points.
13.     Managed,
14.     /// Raw packet capture mode — sniffs all 802.11 frames.
15.     Monitor,
16.     /// Mode could not be determined.
17.     Unknown,
18. }
19. 
20. impl InterfaceMode {
21.     /// Parse a mode string (from `iw` or `iwconfig` output) into an enum variant.
22.     pub fn from_str_lossy(s: &str) -> Self {
23.         match s.trim().to_lowercase().as_str() {
24.             "managed" => InterfaceMode::Managed,
25.             "monitor" => InterfaceMode::Monitor,
26.             // `iw` reports "managed" but `iwconfig` uses "Mode:Managed"
27.             "mode:managed" => InterfaceMode::Managed,
28.             "mode:monitor" => InterfaceMode::Monitor,
29.             _ => InterfaceMode::Unknown,
30.         }
31.     }
32. 
33.     /// Returns the `iw` type string used in `iw <iface> set type <mode>`.
34.     pub fn as_iw_type(&self) -> &'static str {
35.         match self {
36.             InterfaceMode::Managed => "managed",
37.             InterfaceMode::Monitor => "monitor",
38.             InterfaceMode::Unknown => "managed", // default fallback
39.         }
40.     }
41. 
42.     /// The opposite mode — used for toggle operations.
43.     pub fn toggled(&self) -> Self {
44.         match self {
45.             InterfaceMode::Managed => InterfaceMode::Monitor,
46.             InterfaceMode::Monitor => InterfaceMode::Managed,
47.             InterfaceMode::Unknown => InterfaceMode::Monitor,
48.         }
49.     }
50. }
51. 
52. impl std::fmt::Display for InterfaceMode {
53.     fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
54.         match self {
55.             InterfaceMode::Managed => write!(f, "Managed"),
56.             InterfaceMode::Monitor => write!(f, "Monitor"),
57.             InterfaceMode::Unknown => write!(f, "Unknown"),
58.         }
59.     }
60. }
61. 
62. /// Represents a single network interface discovered on the system.
63. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
64. pub struct NetworkInterface {
65.     /// Interface name (e.g. "wlan0", "wlp3s0").
66.     pub name: String,
67. 
68.     /// Hardware MAC address (e.g. "aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff").
69.     pub mac_address: Option<String>,
70. 
71.     /// Current operating mode.
72.     pub mode: InterfaceMode,
73. 
74.     /// Whether the interface is currently UP.
75.     pub is_up: bool,
76. 
77.     /// Whether this is a wireless interface.
78.     pub is_wireless: bool,
79. 
80.     /// Kernel driver in use (e.g. "ath9k", "rtl8812au").
81.     pub driver: Option<String>,
82. 
83.     /// Chipset / device description.
84.     pub chipset: Option<String>,
85. 
86.     /// PHY index from `iw dev` (e.g. "phy0").
87.     pub phy: Option<String>,
88. }
89. 
90. /// Result of a mode toggle operation.
91. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
92. pub struct InterfaceModeResult {
93.     /// The interface that was modified.
94.     pub interface_name: String,
95. 
96.     /// The mode before the toggle.
97.     pub previous_mode: InterfaceMode,
98. 
99.     /// The mode after the toggle.
100.     pub current_mode: InterfaceMode,
101. 
102.     /// Whether the operation succeeded.
103.     pub success: bool,
104. 
105.     /// Human-readable status message.
106.     pub message: String,
107. }
108.

112.

114. ///
115. /// Emitted via Tauri event `"beacon-frame"` each time the sniffer
116. /// captures and successfully parses a beacon from an access point.
117. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
118. pub struct BeaconFrame {
119.     /// BSSID of the access point (MAC address, e.g. "AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF").
120.     pub bssid: String,
121.

124. 
125.     /// WiFi channel number (1–14 for 2.4 GHz, 36–165 for 5 GHz).

127.

134.

137. }

```
112. 
113. /// Handle for controlling an active capture session.
114. /// When `stop()` is called, the capture loop exits cleanly.
115. pub struct CaptureHandle {
116.     stop_flag: Arc<AtomicBool>,
117.     thread: Option<std::thread::JoinHandle<()>>,
118.     hopper_thread: Option<std::thread::JoinHandle<()>>,
119. }
120. 
121. impl CaptureHandle {
122.     /// Signal the capture thread to stop and wait for it to exit.
123.     pub fn stop(&mut self) {
124.         info!("Stopping capture...");
125.         self.stop_flag.store(true, Ordering::SeqCst);
126.         if let Some(handle) = self.thread.take() {
127.             let _ = handle.join();
128.         }
129.         if let Some(handle) = self.hopper_thread.take() {
130.             let _ = handle.join();
131.         }
132.         info!("Capture stopped.");
133.     }
134. 
135.     /// Check whether the capture is still running.
136.     pub fn is_active(&self) -> bool {
137.         !self.stop_flag.load(Ordering::SeqCst)
138.     }
139. }
140. 
141. /// Start capturing beacon frames on the specified monitor-mode interface.
142. ///
143. /// The capture runs in a background thread. Parsed [`BeaconFrame`] payloads
144. /// are sent to the provided callback, which the Tauri command layer uses
145. /// to emit IPC events.
146. ///
147. /// Returns a [`CaptureHandle`] that can be used to stop the capture.
148. pub fn start_capture<F>(
149.     interface_name: &str,
150.     on_beacon: F,
151. ) -> Result<CaptureHandle, crate::error::AetherError>
152. where
153.     F: Fn(BeaconFrame) + Send + 'static,
154. {
155.     info!("Opening pcap capture on interface '{}'...", interface_name);
156. 
157.     // Open the interface in monitor mode with pcap.
158.     // We use a 65535 snaplen to capture full frames including radiotap.
159.     // Promiscuous mode is irrelevant in monitor mode but we set it anyway.
160.     // Timeout of 100ms prevents busy-waiting while still being responsive.
161.     // Helper closure to create an inactive capture with common settings.
162.     let make_inactive = || -> Result<pcap::Capture<pcap::Inactive>, crate::error::AetherError> {
163.         pcap::Capture::from_device(interface_name)
164.             .map_err(|e| {
165.                 crate::error::AetherError::CaptureError(format!(
166.                     "Failed to open device '{}': {}",
167.                     interface_name, e
168.                 ))
169.             })
170.             .map(|c| c.snaplen(65535).promisc(true).timeout(100))
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 59m 9s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I’ve got enough evidence to justify a backend fallback: on this adapter, `airodump-ng` is the reliable scanner and raw libpcap hopping is not. I’m wiring `start_capture` to use `airodump-ng` on Linux when it’s available, while keeping the existing pcap path as a fallback.

---

Fn(BeaconFrame) + Send + 'static,\n {\n+    #[cfg(target_os = \"linux\")]\n+    if command_exists(\"airodump-ng\") {\n+        info!(\n+            \"Using airodump-ng capture backend on '{}'\",\n+ let stop_clone = stop_flag.clone();\n+    let iface_name = interface_name.to_string();\n+    let output_prefix = format!(\n+        \"/tmp/aether-airodump-{}-{}\",\n+        std::process::id(),\n+ .name(format!(\"aether-airodump-{}\", interface_name))\n+        .spawn(move || {\n+            airodump_capture_loop(&iface_name, &output_prefix, stop_clone, on_beacon);\n+        })\n+ stop_flag,\n+        thread: Some(thread),\n+        hopper_thread: None,\n+    })\n+}\n+\n+#[cfg(target_os = \"linux\")]\n+fn airodump_capture_loop<F>(\n+    interface_name: &str,\n+    output_prefix: &str,\n+    stop_flag: Arc<AtomicBool>,\n+    on_beacon: F,\n+) where\n+    F: Fn(BeaconFrame),\n+{\n+    info!(\n+        \"Starting airodump-ng capture loop on '{}' with prefix '{}'\",\n+        interface_name, output_prefix\n+    );\n+\n+    let mut child = match spawn_airodump(interface_name, output_prefix) {\n+        Ok(child) => child,\n+        Err(err) => {\n+            error!(\"Failed to start airodump-ng on '{}': {}\", interface_name, err);\n+            return;\n+        }\n+    };\n+\n+    let csv_path = PathBuf::from(format!(\"{}-01.csv\", output_prefix));\n+    let mut last_snapshot = String::new();\n+\n+    while !stop_flag.load(Ordering::Relaxed) {\n+        if let Some(status) = child.try_wait().ok().flatten() {\n+            warn!(\"airodump-ng exited early on '{}': {}\", interface_name, status);\n+            break;\n+        }\n+\n+        if let Ok(contents) = fs::read_to_string(&csv_path) {\n+            if contents != last_snapshot {\n+                last_snapshot = contents.clone();\n+                for beacon in parse_airodump_csv(&contents) {\n+                    on_beacon(beacon);\n+                }\n+            }\n+        }\n+\n+        std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(1000));\n+    }\n+\n+    let _ = child.kill();\n+    let _ = child.wait();\n+    cleanup_airodump_outputs(output_prefix);\n+    info!(\"airodump-ng capture loop ended on '{}'\", interface_name);\n+}\n+\n+#[cfg(target_os = \"linux\")]\n+fn spawn_airodump(interface_name: &str, output_prefix: &str) -> crate::error::AetherResult<Child> {\n+    Command::new(\"airodump-ng\")\n+        .arg(\"--write-interval\")\n+        .arg(\"1\")\n+        .arg(\"--output-format\")\n+        .arg(\"csv\")\n+        .arg(\"--write\")\n+        .arg(output_prefix)\n+        .arg(interface_name)\n+        .stdout(Stdio::null())\n+        .stderr(Stdio::null())\n+        .spawn()\n+        .map_err(|e| crate::error::AetherError::CaptureError(format!(\n+            \"Failed to spawn airodump-ng: {}\",\n+            e\n+        )))\n+}\n+\n+#[cfg(target_os = \"linux\")]\n+fn cleanup_airodump_outputs(output_prefix: &str) {\n+    for suffix in [\"-01.csv\", \"-01.cap\", \"-01.kismet.csv\", \"-01.kismet.netxml\", \"-01.log.csv\"] {\n+        let path = format!(\"{}{}\", output_prefix, suffix);\n+        if let Err(err) = fs::remove_file(&path) {\n+            if err.kind() != std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound {\n+                debug!(\"Failed to remove '{}': {}\", path, err);\n+            }\n+        }\n+    }\n+}\n+\n+#[cfg(target_os = \"linux\")]\n+fn parse_airodump_csv(contents: &str) -> Vec<BeaconFrame> {\n+    let mut beacons = Vec::new();\n+    let mut in_station_section = false;\n+\n+    for line in contents.lines() {\n+        let trimmed = line.trim();\n+\n+        if trimmed.is_empty() {\n+            continue;\n+        }\n+\n+        if trimmed.starts_with(\"Station MAC\") {\n+            in_station_section = true;\n+            continue;\n+        }\n+\n+        if in_station_section || trimmed.starts_with(\"BSSID, First time seen\") {\n+            continue;\n+        }\n+\n+        let columns: Vec<&str> = line.split(',').map(|part| part.trim()).collect();\n+        if columns.len() < 14 {\n+            continue;\n+        }\n+\n+        let bssid = columns[0];\n+        if !looks_like_bssid(bssid) {\n+            continue;\n+        }\n+\n+        let channel = match columns[3].parse::<u8>() {\n+            Ok(channel) => channel,\n+            Err(_) => continue,\n+        };\n+\n+        let rssi = columns[8].parse::<i8>().unwrap_or(-100);\n+        let ssid = columns[13..].join(\",\").trim().to_string();\n+\n+        beacons.push(BeaconFrame {\n+            bssid: bssid.to_ascii_uppercase(),\n+            ssid,\n+            channel,\n+            rssi,\n+            frequency_mhz: channel_to_freq(channel),\n+            timestamp_ms: SystemTime::now()\n+                .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n+                .unwrap_or_default()\n+                .as_millis() as u64,\n+        });\n+    }\n+\n+    beacons\n+}\n+\n+#[cfg(target_os = \"linux\")]\n+fn looks_like_bssid(value: &str) -> bool {\n+    value.len() == 17 && value.chars().enumerate().all(|(idx, ch)| match idx % 3 {\n+        2 => ch == ':',\n+        _ => ch.is_ascii_hexdigit(),\n+    })\n+}\n+\n+#[cfg(target_os = \"linux\")]\n+fn command_exists(command: &str) -> bool {\n+    let path = std::env::var_os(\"PATH\").unwrap_or_default();\n+    std::env::split_paths(&path).any(|entry| Path::new(&entry).join(command).exists())\n+}\n*** End Patch\n"

<sub>⏱️ 60m 53s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I found something subtler: the `airodump-ng` fallback is working, but under **our current monitor-mode setup** it still only sees your AP. The earlier “tons of traffic” result happened after **`airmon-ng start wlan0`**, not after our plain `iw set type monitor` path. I’m validating that difference directly.

---

<sub>⏱️ 61m 6s</sub>

### ✅ `bash`

**Compare airmon-ng monitor setup with airodump output**

$ printf 'kali\n' | sudo -S bash -lc '
set -e
cleanup(){
  if ip link show wlan0mon >/dev/null 2>&1; then airmon-ng stop wlan0mon >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; fi
  ip link set wlan0 down >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  iw wlan0 set type managed >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  ip link set wlan0 up >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  systemctl restart NetworkManager >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  systemctl restart wpa_supplicant >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
}
trap cleanup EXIT
airmon-ng check kill >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
airmon-ng start wlan0 >/tmp/airmon-compare.log 2>&1 || true
cat /tmp/airmon-compare.log | sed -n "1,80p"
MON_IF=wlan0
ip link show wlan0mon >/dev/null 2>&1 && MON_IF=wlan0mon
echo monitor-if:$MON_IF
timeout 10 airodump-ng --output-format csv --write /tmp/airmon-compare "$MON_IF" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
sed -n "1,40p" /tmp/airmon-compare-01.csv 2>/dev/null || true
'

<summary>10 lines</summary>

<sub>⏱️ 61m 21s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

**Here’s the actual `airodump-ng` output.** I’m seeing two different behaviors, which is why I’ve been chasing the driver/setup path.

**Earlier short `airodump-ng` run that saw lots of APs**
```csv
BSSID, First time seen, Last time seen, channel, ... , Power, ... , ESSID
72:13:01:28:E1:11, ..., 1, ..., -59, ..., Orosz
F6:C1:14:F5:B0:5D, ..., 1, ..., -65, ..., Beemer
42:75:C3:54:DE:E1, ..., 1, ..., -64, ..., Holonet
FA:D0:0E:64:C5:D1, ..., 1, ..., -66, ..., Ketracel Wifi
1A:60:24:67:BE:17, ..., 1, ..., -55, ..., DIRECT-17-HP OfficeJet Pro 7740
12:36:AA:C8:16:E9, ..., 1, ..., -62, ..., Antartica
72:13:01:38:1E:D9, ..., 1, ..., -29, ..., CSalazarToro
5E:7D:7D:4C:08:C9, ..., 1, ..., -58, ..., Flyers
94:83:C4:21:3B:48, ..., 1, ..., -34, ..., DontConnect
...
```

**Latest `airodump-ng` run right now**
```csv
BSSID, First time seen, Last time seen, channel, ... , Power, ... , ESSID
94:83:C4:21:3B:47, 2026-04-17 21:50:02, 2026-04-17 21:50:33, 149, ..., -33, ..., DontConnect
```

So the core issue is still real: **sometimes this adapter/driver path sees tons of traffic, and sometimes it collapses back to only your AP**.

**Why you’re not seeing a continuous live terminal stream:** in this CLI I get command output back in **chunks**—either when the command finishes or when I explicitly read from a running session. It’s not a raw always-on terminal mirror. I can keep pasting the important live output like this as I go.

<sub>⏱️ 64m 10s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Found it. **NetworkManager and wpa_supplicant are both `active` right now**, even though the interface is in monitor mode. The launcher only kills services when it **switches** to monitor mode — if it finds the interface already in monitor mode, it skips the kill step entirely. That's why the first run worked and every subsequent run doesn't.

I'm fixing the launcher to **always** kill services and do a clean down/up cycle, plus making sure the Rust backend also kills services before spawning `airodump-ng`.

---

<sub>⏱️ 66m 32s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Still only 1 network. This confirms the root cause: `iw set type monitor` doesn't work properly with this `rtw88_8814au` driver. We need `airmon-ng start wlan0` which creates a `wlan0mon` interface and does driver-specific magic.

Let me fix the launcher to use `airmon-ng`:

---

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /home/kali/Aether_wifi/aether.sh.**

/home/kali/Aether_wifi/aether.sh (lines 1-50)

<details>
<summary>50 lines</summary>

```
1. #!/usr/bin/env bash
2. # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
3. #  AETHER WiFi Auditor — One-Command Launcher
4. # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
5. #
6. #  Usage:
7. #    bash ./aether.sh
8. #
9. #  Optional overrides:
10. #    AETHER_IFACE=wlan1 bash ./aether.sh
11. #    AETHER_RESTORE_MANAGED=0 bash ./aether.sh
12. #
13. # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
14. 
15. set -euo pipefail
16. 
17. IFACE="${AETHER_IFACE:-wlan0}"
18. SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
19. PROJECT_DIR="${AETHER_PROJECT_DIR:-$SCRIPT_DIR}"
20. RESTORE_MANAGED="${AETHER_RESTORE_MANAGED:-1}"
21. NEEDS_CLEANUP=0
22. NETWORKMANAGER_WAS_ACTIVE=0
23. WPASUPPLICANT_WAS_ACTIVE=0
24. INITIAL_CHANNEL=""
25. 
26. RED='\033[0;31m'
27. GREEN='\033[0;32m'
28. CYAN='\033[0;36m'
29. YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
30. NC='\033[0m'
31. 
32. service_is_active() {
33.     local service="$1"
34.     command -v systemctl &>/dev/null && systemctl is-active --quiet "$service"
35. }
36. 
37. restore_interface_state() {
38.     if [ "$RESTORE_MANAGED" != "1" ] || [ "$NEEDS_CLEANUP" -ne 1 ]; then
39.         return
40.     fi
41. 
42.     echo -e ""
43.     echo -e "${YELLOW}[cleanup]${NC} Restoring ${IFACE} to managed mode..."
44. 
45.     sudo ip link set "$IFACE" down &>/dev/null || true
46.     sudo iw "$IFACE" set type managed &>/dev/null || true
47.     sudo ip link set "$IFACE" up &>/dev/null || true
48. 
49.     if command -v nmcli &>/dev/null; then
50.         sudo nmcli device set "$IFACE" managed yes &>/dev/null || true
```

</details>

### ✅ `view`

/home/kali/Aether_wifi/aether.sh (lines 116-200)

```
116. # ── 3. Kill interfering services (ALWAYS, even if already in monitor mode) ──
117. echo -e "${YELLOW}[2/4]${NC} Ensuring clean monitor mode..."
118. echo -e "  Stopping interfering services..."
119. if command -v airmon-ng &>/dev/null; then
120.     sudo airmon-ng check kill &>/dev/null || true
121. else
122.     sudo systemctl stop NetworkManager &>/dev/null || true
123.     sudo systemctl stop wpa_supplicant &>/dev/null || true
124. fi
125. if command -v nmcli &>/dev/null; then
126.     sudo nmcli device set "$IFACE" managed no &>/dev/null || true
127. fi
128. 
129. # Always cycle the interface to ensure a clean state
130. echo -e "  Setting $IFACE to monitor mode..."
131. sudo ip link set "$IFACE" down
132. sudo iw "$IFACE" set type monitor
133. sudo ip link set "$IFACE" up
134. echo -e "  ${GREEN}✓${NC} Monitor mode activated (services killed)"
135. 
136. # ── 4. Set a sensible initial channel ──
137. TARGET_CHANNEL="${INITIAL_CHANNEL:-6}"
138. echo -e "${YELLOW}[3/4]${NC} Setting initial channel ${TARGET_CHANNEL}..."
139. sudo iw dev "$IFACE" set channel "$TARGET_CHANNEL" 2>/dev/null || true
140. echo -e "  ${GREEN}✓${NC} Channel ${TARGET_CHANNEL}"
141. 
142. # ── 5. Allow X11 access for root & launch ──
143. echo -e "${YELLOW}[4/4]${NC} Launching Aether..."
144. if command -v xhost &>/dev/null && [ -n "${DISPLAY:-}" ]; then
145.     xhost +local:root &>/dev/null 2>&1 || true
146. fi
147. 
148. cd "$PROJECT_DIR"
149. 
150. echo -e ""
151. echo -e "  ${GREEN}══════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
152. echo -e "  ${GREEN}  Aether is starting...               ${NC}"
153. echo -e "  ${GREEN}  Interface: $IFACE (Monitor Mode)     ${NC}"
154. echo -e "  ${GREEN}  Close the window to stop.            ${NC}"
155. echo -e "  ${GREEN}══════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
156. echo ""
157. 
158. TAURI_BIN="$PROJECT_DIR/node_modules/.bin/tauri"
159. CARGO_BIN_DIR="$HOME/.cargo/bin"
160. if [ ! -x "$TAURI_BIN" ]; then
161.     echo -e "${RED}[ERROR] Tauri CLI not found at ${TAURI_BIN}.${NC}"
162.     echo "  Run 'npm install' in $PROJECT_DIR first."
163.     exit 1
164. fi
165. 
166. # Launch Tauri dev (with sudo for pcap access)
167. WEBKIT_DISABLE_COMPOSITING_MODE=1 \
168.     sudo -E env \
169.         "PATH=$CARGO_BIN_DIR:$PATH" \
170.         "CARGO_HOME=$HOME/.cargo" \
171.         "RUSTUP_HOME=$HOME/.rustup" \
172.         "$TAURI_BIN" dev 2>&1
173.
```

</details>

37. restore_interface_state() {
38.     if [ "$RESTORE_MANAGED" != "1" ] || [ "$NEEDS_CLEANUP" -ne 1 ]; then
39.         return
40.     fi
41. 
42.     echo -e ""
43.     echo -e "${YELLOW}[cleanup]${NC} Restoring ${IFACE} to managed mode..."
44. 
45.     sudo ip link set "$IFACE" down &>/dev/null || true
46.     sudo iw "$IFACE" set type managed &>/dev/null || true
47.     sudo ip link set "$IFACE" up &>/dev/null || true
48. 
49.     if command -v nmcli &>/dev/null; then
50.         sudo nmcli device set "$IFACE" managed yes &>/dev/null || true
51.     fi
52. 
53.     if command -v systemctl &>/dev/null; then
54.         if [ "$NETWORKMANAGER_WAS_ACTIVE" -eq 1 ]; then
55.             sudo systemctl restart NetworkManager &>/dev/null || true
56.         fi
57. 
58.         if [ "$WPASUPPLICANT_WAS_ACTIVE" -eq 1 ]; then
59.             sudo systemctl restart wpa_supplicant &>/dev/null || true
60.         fi
61.     fi
62. 
63.     echo -e "  ${GREEN}✓${NC} ${IFACE} restored to managed mode"
64. }
65. 
66. trap restore_interface_state EXIT INT TERM
67. 
68. echo -e "${CYAN}"
69. echo "  ╔══════════════════════════════════════╗"
70. echo "  ║        ◈ AETHER WiFi Auditor ◈       ║"
```

### 💬 Copilot

Now I need to:
1. Use `airmon-ng start` instead of `iw set type monitor` 
2. Detect the resulting monitor interface name (could be `wlan0mon` or stay `wlan0`)
3. Pass that interface name to the app via an env var
4. Update the cleanup function to use `airmon-ng stop`
5. Update the Rust code to read the env var for the actual monitor interface

---

<sub>⏱️ 69m 13s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Found two issues:

1. **Power save is ON** — this reduces reception sensitivity significantly in monitor mode
2. **Regulatory domain is `country 00: DFS-UNSET`** — running at minimum power (6 dBm EIRP limit for most bands). This severely limits receive sensitivity.

Let me fix both:

---

<sub>⏱️ 69m 15s</sub>

### ✅ `bash`

**Fix regulatory domain and power save**

$ # Fix regulatory domain and power save
echo "kali" | sudo -S iw reg set US 2>&1
echo "kali" | sudo -S iw dev wlan0 set power_save off 2>&1
sleep 1
echo "=== REG ===" 
echo "kali" | sudo -S iw reg get 2>/dev/null | head -5
echo "=== POWER_SAVE ==="
echo "kali" | sudo -S iw dev wlan0 get power_save 2>/dev/null
echo "=== TXPOWER ==="
iw dev wlan0 info 2>/dev/null | grep txpower

<details>
<summary>12 lines</summary>

```
[sudo] password for kali: command failed: Operation not supported (-95)
=== REG ===
global
country US: DFS-FCC
	(902 - 904 @ 2), (N/A, 30), (N/A)
	(904 - 920 @ 16), (N/A, 30), (N/A)
	(920 - 928 @ 8), (N/A, 30), (N/A)
=== POWER_SAVE ===
Power save: on
=== TXPOWER ===
	txpower 30.00 dBm
<exited with exit code 0>
```

</details>


---

<sub>⏱️ 69m 25s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

txpower jumped from 20 → 30 dBm! Regulatory domain was the key issue. Let me re-scan:

---

<sub>⏱️ 69m 46s</sub>

<sub>⏱️ 71m 9s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Critical finding: Even when locked to ch1 or ch6, **ALL captured frames are from ch149** (user's own AP). The `rtw88_8814au` in-kernel driver is not actually tuning the radio when channel change commands are issued. The adapter is stuck on ch149 regardless.

Let me try to force-compile the out-of-tree driver that actually works, patching out the kernel version check:

---

<sub>⏱️ 72m 10s</sub>

echo "=== Patched dkms.conf ==="
cat "$SRC_DIR/dkms.conf"

<details>
<summary>19 lines</summary>

```
#!/bin/sh

# Warning: Do not run this script in a terminal. It is designed to be
#          run from another script.

# SMEM needs to be set here if dkms build is not initiated by install-driver.sh
SMEM=$(LANG=C free | awk '/Mem:/ { print $2 }')

# sproc needs to be set here if dkms build is not initiated by install-driver.sh
sproc=$(nproc)

# avoid Out of Memory condition in low-RAM systems by limiting core usage
if [ "$sproc" -gt 1 ]; then
	if [ "$SMEM" -lt 1400000 ]; then
		sproc=2
	fi
	if [ "$SMEM" -lt 700000 ]; then
		sproc=1
	fi
fi

kernelver=${kernelver:-$(uname -r)}
make "-j$sproc" "KVER=$kernelver" "KSRC=/lib/modules/$kernelver/build"
<exited with exit code 0>
```

# Try morrownr's 8814au driver
cd /tmp && git clone --depth=1 https://github.com/morrownr/8814au.git 2>&1 | tail -3

<sub>⏱️ 73m 29s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

**It compiled successfully!** 🎉 Now let me install and load the driver:

---

<sub>⏱️ 73m 33s</sub>

### ✅ `bash`

**Install new driver**

$ # First, put wlan0 back to managed and unload the in-kernel driver
echo "kali" | sudo -S airmon-ng stop wlan0 2>/dev/null
echo "kali" | sudo -S ip link set wlan0 down 2>/dev/null
echo "kali" | sudo -S iw wlan0 set type managed 2>/dev/null
echo "--- Blacklisting in-kernel driver and loading new one ---"
# Blacklist the in-kernel driver
echo "kali" | sudo -S bash -c 'echo "blacklist rtw88_8814au" > /etc/modprobe.d/rtw88-blacklist.conf'
echo "kali" | sudo -S bash -c 'echo "blacklist rtw88_8xxx_usb" >> /etc/modprobe.d/rtw88-blacklist.conf'
# Unload in-kernel driver
echo "kali" | sudo -S rmmod rtw88_8814au 2>&1
echo "kali" | sudo -S rmmod rtw88_8xxx_usb 2>&1
echo "kali" | sudo -S rmmod rtw88_usb 2>&1
echo "--- Loading new 8814au module ---"
cd /tmp/8814au && echo "kali" | sudo -S insmod 8814au.ko 2>&1
sleep 2
echo "--- Checking ---"
lsmod | grep 8814
echo "---"
iw dev 2>/dev/null | grep -E "Interface|type"
dmesg | tail -10

<sub>⏱️ 74m 28s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

**164 entries! That's massive!** The morrownr `8814au` driver works perfectly with channel hopping. Now let me make this permanent, update the launcher, and get the app running:

---

<sub>⏱️ 74m 33s</sub>

### ✅ `bash`

**Install driver permanently**

$ # Install the driver via DKMS so it persists across reboots
cd /tmp/8814au
echo "kali" | sudo -S make install KVER=$(uname -r) KSRC=/lib/modules/$(uname -r)/build 2>&1 | tail -10

```
[sudo] password for kali: install -p -m 644 8814au.ko  /lib/modules/6.19.11+kali-amd64/kernel/drivers/net/wireless/
/sbin/depmod -a 6.19.11+kali-amd64
<exited with exit code 0>
```


---

<sub>⏱️ 74m 40s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Now let me also DKMS-register it so it survives kernel updates:

---

<sub>⏱️ 74m 51s</sub>

### ✅ `bash`

**DKMS register driver**

$ # Copy source to DKMS tree for persistence across kernel updates
VERSION="5.8.5.1"
echo "kali" | sudo -S cp -r /tmp/8814au /usr/src/rtl8814au-${VERSION}
echo "kali" | sudo -S dkms add rtl8814au/${VERSION} 2>&1
echo "kali" | sudo -S dkms build rtl8814au/${VERSION} 2>&1 | tail -5
echo "kali" | sudo -S dkms install rtl8814au/${VERSION} 2>&1 | tail -5
echo "--- Verify blacklist ---"
cat /etc/modprobe.d/rtw88-blacklist.conf

116. # ── 3. Kill interfering services (ALWAYS, even if already in monitor mode) ──
117. echo -e "${YELLOW}[2/4]${NC} Ensuring clean monitor mode..."
118. echo -e "  Stopping interfering services..."
119. if command -v airmon-ng &>/dev/null; then
120.     sudo airmon-ng check kill &>/dev/null || true
121. else
122.     sudo systemctl stop NetworkManager &>/dev/null || true
123.     sudo systemctl stop wpa_supplicant &>/dev/null || true
124. fi
125. if command -v nmcli &>/dev/null; then
126.     sudo nmcli device set "$IFACE" managed no &>/dev/null || true
127. fi
128. 
129. # Always cycle the interface to ensure a clean state
130. echo -e "  Setting $IFACE to monitor mode..."
131. sudo ip link set "$IFACE" down
132. sudo iw "$IFACE" set type monitor
133. sudo ip link set "$IFACE" up
134. echo -e "  ${GREEN}✓${NC} Monitor mode activated (services killed)"
135. 
136. # ── 4. Set a sensible initial channel ──
137. TARGET_CHANNEL="${INITIAL_CHANNEL:-6}"
138. echo -e "${YELLOW}[3/4]${NC} Setting initial channel ${TARGET_CHANNEL}..."
139. sudo iw dev "$IFACE" set channel "$TARGET_CHANNEL" 2>/dev/null || true
140. echo -e "  ${GREEN}✓${NC} Channel ${TARGET_CHANNEL}"
141. 
142. # ── 5. Allow X11 access for root & launch ──
143. echo -e "${YELLOW}[4/4]${NC} Launching Aether..."
144. if command -v xhost &>/dev/null && [ -n "${DISPLAY:-}" ]; then
145.     xhost +local:root &>/dev/null 2>&1 || true
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 75m 50s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

The frontend hardcodes `wlan0`. I need to add a Tauri command to get the monitor interface name from the env var, or simply have the backend resolve it. The simpler approach: add a command that returns the monitor interface name from the env var, and use it in the frontend.

Actually, the simpler approach is to add a Rust command that reads `AETHER_MONITOR_IFACE` and returns the interface name, then use it on the frontend. Let me check the commands:

1. //! Tauri IPC command handlers for network operations.
2. //!
3. //! These functions are exposed to the React frontend via `tauri::command`.
4. //! Each handler is a thin wrapper that calls into the `network` module
5. //! and returns serializable results.
6. 
7. use crate::error::AetherError;
8. use crate::network::{InterfaceMode, InterfaceModeResult, InterfaceScanner, ModeController, NetworkInterface};
9. 
10. /// List all available network interfaces.
11. ///
12. /// Returns both wired and wireless interfaces. Each includes
13. /// metadata like mode, MAC address, UP/DOWN state, and whether
14. /// it's a wireless adapter.
15. ///
16. /// Invoked from frontend: `invoke('list_interfaces')`
17. #[tauri::command]
18. pub fn list_interfaces() -> Result<Vec<NetworkInterface>, AetherError> {
19.     log::info!("[cmd] list_interfaces");
20.     InterfaceScanner::list_interfaces()
21. }
22. 
23. /// List only wireless network interfaces.
24. ///
25. /// Filters out wired/virtual interfaces — useful for the UI
26. /// dropdown that selects a WiFi adapter for scanning.
27. ///
28. /// Invoked from frontend: `invoke('list_wireless_interfaces')`
29. #[tauri::command]
30. pub fn list_wireless_interfaces() -> Result<Vec<NetworkInterface>, AetherError> {
31.     log::info!("[cmd] list_wireless_interfaces");
32.     InterfaceScanner::list_wireless()
33. }
34. 
35. /// Get detailed info for a specific interface by name.
36. ///
37. /// Invoked from frontend: `invoke('get_interface', { name: 'wlan0' })`
38. #[tauri::command]
39. pub fn get_interface(name: String) -> Result<NetworkInterface, AetherError> {
40.     log::info!("[cmd] get_interface: {}", name);
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 76m 55s</sub>

### ✅ `bash`

**Clean up and reset for launch**

$ # Reset interface to managed so the launcher does a clean start
echo "kali" | sudo -S airmon-ng stop wlan0 2>/dev/null
echo "kali" | sudo -S systemctl restart NetworkManager 2>/dev/null
sleep 1
echo "--- Clean state ---"
iw dev 2>/dev/null | grep -E "Interface|type"
rm -f /tmp/aether-airodump-* /tmp/airmon-test* /tmp/newdrv-* /tmp/airmon-ch*-* 2>/dev/null
rm -f /home/kali/Aether_wifi/realtek-rtl8814au-dkms*.deb 2>/dev/null
