1. import { Crosshair, Radio, Square } from "lucide-react";
2. import ReactECharts from "echarts-for-react";
3. import { useMemo, useState } from "react";
4. import { useBeaconCapture } from "../hooks/useBeaconCapture";
5. 
6. export function Hunt() {
7.     const [targetMac, setTargetMac] = useState("");
8.     const [channelLock, setChannelLock] = useState("Auto-Scan");
9.     const { beaconStream, isCapturing, startCapture, stopCapture } = useBeaconCapture();
10. 
11.     const handleToggleCapture = () => {
12.         if (isCapturing) stopCapture();
13.         else startCapture('wlan0'); // default capturing interface
14.     };
15. 
16.     const cleanMac = targetMac.trim().toLowerCase();
17. 
18.     // Data specifically for the target MAC
19.     const targetData = useMemo(() => {
20.         if (!cleanMac) return [];
21.         return beaconStream.filter(b => b.bssid.toLowerCase() === cleanMac);
22.     }, [beaconStream, cleanMac]);
23. 
24.     const latestRssi = targetData.length > 0 ? targetData[targetData.length - 1].rssi : null;
25. 
26.     const chartOptions = useMemo(() => {
27.         const now = Date.now();
28.         const sixtySecondsAgo = now - 60000;
29. 
30.         // Map data to x,y format, only keeping last 60 seconds
31.         const seriesData = targetData
32.             .filter(b => b.timestamp_ms >= sixtySecondsAgo)
33.             .map(b => [b.timestamp_ms, b.rssi]);
34. 
35.         return {
36.             backgroundColor: 'transparent',
37.             tooltip: { trigger: 'axis' },
38.             xAxis: {
39.                 type: 'time',
40.                 min: sixtySecondsAgo,
41.                 max: now,
42.                 splitLine: { show: false },
43.                 axisLine: { lineStyle: { color: '#444' } },
44.                 axisLabel: { color: '#888', formatter: '{mm}:{ss}' }
45.             },
46.             yAxis: {
47.                 type: 'value',
48.                 min: -100,
49.                 max: -20,
50.                 name: 'RSSI (dBm)',
51.                 splitLine: { lineStyle: { color: '#ef4444', opacity: 0.1 } },
52.                 axisLine: { lineStyle: { color: '#444' } },
53.                 axisLabel: { color: '#ef4444' }
54.             },
55.             series: [{
56.                 name: 'Target RSSI',
57.                 type: 'line',
58.                 smooth: true,
59.                 showSymbol: false,
60.                 lineStyle: { width: 3, color: '#ef4444' },
61.                 areaStyle: {
62.                     color: {
63.                         type: 'linear', x: 0, y: 0, x2: 0, y2: 1,
64.                         colorStops: [{ offset: 0, color: 'rgba(239, 68, 68, 0.5)' }, { offset: 1, color: 'rgba(239, 68, 68, 0)' }]
65.                     }
66.                 },
67.                 data: seriesData
68.             }],
69.             animation: false
70.         };
71.     }, [targetData]);
72. 
73.     return (
74.         <div className="h-full flex flex-col animate-in fade-in slide-in-from-bottom-4 duration-500">
75.             <header className="mb-8 flex justify-between items-start">
76.                 <div>
77.                     <h1 className="text-3xl font-mono font-bold tracking-tight text-foreground flex items-center gap-3">
78.                         <Crosshair className="w-8 h-8 text-destructive" />
79.                         <span className="text-glow text-destructive">HUNT</span> <span className="opacity-50 text-muted-foreground font-sans text-2xl font-normal">// TARGETED TRACKING</span>
80.                     </h1>
81.                     <p className="text-muted-foreground mt-2 font-mono text-sm uppercase tracking-wider">RSSI Tracker & Direction Finding</p>
82.                 </div>
83.             </header>
84. 
85.             <div className="grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-6 h-full">
86.                 {/* Sidebar Settings / Target Selector */}
87.                 <div className="lg:col-span-1 glass-panel rounded-xl p-6 border border-border/40 flex flex-col gap-6">
88.                     <div>
89.                         <label className="text-xs font-mono uppercase tracking-widest text-muted-foreground mb-2 block">Target MAC</label>
90.                         <input
91.                             type="text"
92.                             placeholder="00:11:22:33:44:55"
93.                             value={targetMac}
94.                             onChange={(e) => setTargetMac(e.target.value)}
95.                             className="w-full bg-black/40 border border-border/60 rounded px-4 py-2 font-mono text-sm text-primary focus:border-primary focus:outline-none transition-colors uppercase"
96.                         />
97.                     </div>
98.                     <div>
99.                         <label className="text-xs font-mono uppercase tracking-widest text-muted-foreground mb-2 block">Lock Channel</label>
100.                         <select
101.                             value={channelLock}
102.                             onChange={(e) => setChannelLock(e.target.value)}
103.                             className="w-full bg-black/40 border border-border/60 rounded px-4 py-2 font-mono text-sm text-foreground focus:border-primary focus:outline-none transition-colors appearance-none"
104.                         >
105.                             <option>Auto-Scan</option>
106.                             <option>CH 1 (2.4GHz)</option>
107.                             <option>CH 6 (2.4GHz)</option>
108.                             <option>CH 11 (2.4GHz)</option>
109.                         </select>
110.                     </div>
111.                     <button
112.                         onClick={handleToggleCapture}
113.                         className={`mt-auto w-full transition-all rounded py-3 font-mono text-sm uppercase tracking-widest flex items-center justify-center gap-2 ${isCapturing
114.                             ? "bg-destructive border border-destructive text-destructive-foreground hover:bg-destructive/80"
115.                             : "bg-destructive/10 border border-destructive/50 text-destructive hover:bg-destructive hover:text-destructive-foreground"
116.                             }`}
117.                     >
118.                         {isCapturing ? <><Square className="w-4 h-4" /> Disengage</> : <><Radio className="w-4 h-4" /> Engage Tracker</>}
119.                     </button>
120.                 </div>
121. 
122.                 {/* Main Radar Graph */}
123.                 <div className="lg:col-span-3 glass-panel rounded-xl border border-destructive/30 p-6 flex flex-col relative overflow-hidden">
124.                     {/* Background Scanner Effect */}
125.                     <div className="absolute inset-0 bg-[radial-gradient(circle_at_center,rgba(239,68,68,0.05)_0%,transparent_70%)] pointer-events-none"></div>
126. 
127.                     <div className="flex justify-between items-center mb-6 z-10">
128.                         <h3 className="font-mono text-sm uppercase tracking-widest text-destructive">Signal Strength (dBm)</h3>
129.                         <span className="font-mono text-3xl font-bold text-destructive">
130.                             {latestRssi !== null ? latestRssi : "- --"}
131.                         </span>
132.                     </div>
133. 
134.                     <div className="flex-1 bg-black/40 border border-border/20 rounded relative z-10 p-2">
135.                         {!cleanMac ? (
136.                             <div className="absolute inset-0 flex flex-col items-center justify-center">
137.                                 <div className="w-32 h-32 rounded-full border border-destructive/20 flex items-center justify-center relative overflow-hidden mb-4">
138.                                     <div className="absolute w-full h-[1px] bg-destructive/40 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 animate-pulse-fast pointer-events-none"></div>
139.                                     <div className="absolute h-full w-[1px] bg-destructive/40 left-1/2 -translate-x-1/2 animate-pulse-fast pointer-events-none"></div>
140.                                 </div>
141.                                 <span className="font-mono text-sm text-destructive/50 uppercase tracking-widest">No Target MAC Specified</span>
142.                             </div>
143.                         ) : (
144.                             <ReactECharts
145.                                 option={chartOptions}
146.                                 style={{ height: '100%', width: '100%' }}
147.                                 notMerge={true}
148.                                 lazyUpdate={true}
149.                             />
150.                         )}
151.                     </div>
152.                 </div>
153.             </div>
154.         </div>
155.     );
156. }
157.
```

1. /**
2.  * Aether — useBeaconCapture Hook
3.  *
4.  * A React hook that manages the lifecycle of a packet capture session,
5.  * providing a real-time stream of parsed 802.11 Beacon frames.
6.  *
7.  * @example
8.  * ```tsx
9.  * function SpectrumView() {
10.  *   const { beacons, isCapturing, error, startCapture, stopCapture } =
11.  *     useBeaconCapture();
12.  *
13.  *   return (
14.  *     <div>
15.  *       <button onClick={() => startCapture('wlan0mon')}>Start</button>
16.  *       <button onClick={stopCapture}>Stop</button>
17.  *       {isCapturing && <span>Capturing...</span>}
18.  *       {error && <span>Error: {error}</span>}
19.  *       <ul>
20.  *         {Array.from(beacons.values()).map((b) => (
21.  *           <li key={b.bssid}>
22.  *             {b.ssid || '(hidden)'} — CH{b.channel} — {b.rssi}dBm
23.  *           </li>
24.  *         ))}
25.  *       </ul>
26.  *     </div>
27.  *   );
28.  * }
29.  * ```
30.  */
31. 
32. import { useCallback, useEffect, useRef, useState, createContext, useContext, ReactNode } from 'react';
33. import { invoke } from '@tauri-apps/api/core';
34. import { listen, type UnlistenFn } from '@tauri-apps/api/event';
35. import type { BeaconFrame, CaptureStatus } from '../types/capture';
36. 
37. /**
38.  * Return type of the `useBeaconCapture` hook.
39.  */
40. export interface UseBeaconCaptureResult {
41.     /** Map of BSSID → latest BeaconFrame (deduped, most recent per AP) */
42.     beacons: Map<string, BeaconFrame>;
43. 
44.     /** Ordered list of all beacon events received (for time-series views) */
45.     beaconStream: BeaconFrame[];
46. 
47.     /** Whether a capture is currently active */
48.     isCapturing: boolean;
49. 
50.     /** Error message from the last failed operation, or null */
51.     error: string | null;
52. 
53.     /** Start capturing on the given interface */
54.     startCapture: (interfaceName: string) => Promise<void>;
55. 
56.     /** Stop the current capture session */
57.     stopCapture: () => Promise<void>;
58. }
59. 
60. /**
61.  * Hook for managing real-time 802.11 beacon capture.
62.  *
63.  * - Deduplicates beacons by BSSID (keeps latest reading)
64.  * - Provides both a deduplicated map and a raw stream array
65.  * - Auto-cleans up the Tauri event listener on unmount
66.  *
67.  * @param maxStreamLength Max entries to keep in `beaconStream` (default: 1000)
68.  */
69. function useBeaconCaptureInternal(maxStreamLength = 1000): UseBeaconCaptureResult {
70.     const [beacons, setBeacons] = useState<Map<string, BeaconFrame>>(new Map());
71.     const [beaconStream, setBeaconStream] = useState<BeaconFrame[]>([]);
72.     const [isCapturing, setIsCapturing] = useState(false);
73.     const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);
74. 
75.     // Refs for the cleanup function
76.     const unlistenRef = useRef<UnlistenFn | null>(null);
77.     const streamRef = useRef<BeaconFrame[]>([]);
78.     const beaconsRef = useRef<Map<string, BeaconFrame>>(new Map());
79. 
80.     // Throttle state updates to avoid excessive re-renders
81.     const updateTimerRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null>(null);
82.     const pendingRef = useRef(false);
83. 
84.     const flushUpdates = useCallback(() => {
85.         setBeacons(new Map(beaconsRef.current));
86.         setBeaconStream([...streamRef.current]);
87.         pendingRef.current = false;
88.         updateTimerRef.current = null;
89.     }, []);
90. 
91.     const scheduleUpdate = useCallback(() => {
92.         if (!pendingRef.current) {
93.             pendingRef.current = true;
94.             // Batch updates every 250ms to keep the UI smooth at 60fps
95.             updateTimerRef.current = setTimeout(flushUpdates, 250);
96.         }
97.     }, [flushUpdates]);
98. 
99.     const startCapture = useCallback(
100.         async (interfaceName: string) => {
101.             try {
102.                 setError(null);
103. 
104.                 // Set up the event listener BEFORE starting capture to not miss frames
105.                 const unlisten = await listen<BeaconFrame>('beacon-frame', (event) => {
106.                     const beacon = event.payload;
107. 
108.                     // Update deduplicated map (keyed by BSSID)
109.                     beaconsRef.current.set(beacon.bssid, beacon);
110. 
111.                     // Append to stream (ring buffer)
112.                     streamRef.current.push(beacon);
113.                     if (streamRef.current.length > maxStreamLength) {
114.                         streamRef.current = streamRef.current.slice(-maxStreamLength);
115.                     }
116. 
117.                     scheduleUpdate();
118.                 });
119. 
120.                 unlistenRef.current = unlisten;
121. 
122.                 // Start the capture on the Rust backend
123.                 const status = await invoke<CaptureStatus>('start_capture', {
124.                     interfaceName,
125.                 });
126. 
127.                 setIsCapturing(status.active);
128.             } catch (err: unknown) {
129.                 const message =
130.                     err instanceof Error
131.                         ? err.message
132.                         : typeof err === 'string'
133.                             ? err
134.                             : JSON.stringify(err);
135.                 setError(message);
136.                 setIsCapturing(false);
137. 
138.                 // Clean up the listener if capture failed to start
139.                 if (unlistenRef.current) {
140.                     unlistenRef.current();
141.                     unlistenRef.current = null;
142.                 }
143.             }
144.         },
145.         [maxStreamLength, scheduleUpdate]
146.     );
147. 
148.     const stopCapture = useCallback(async () => {
149.         try {
150.             await invoke<CaptureStatus>('stop_capture');
151.             setIsCapturing(false);
152.         } catch (err: unknown) {
153.             const message =
154.                 err instanceof Error
155.                     ? err.message
156.                     : typeof err === 'string'
157.                         ? err
158.                         : JSON.stringify(err);
159.             setError(message);
160.         } finally {
161.             // Always clean up the event listener
162.             if (unlistenRef.current) {
163.                 unlistenRef.current();
164.                 unlistenRef.current = null;
165.             }
166.             if (updateTimerRef.current) {
167.                 clearTimeout(updateTimerRef.current);
168.                 updateTimerRef.current = null;
169.             }
170.         }
171.     }, []);
172. 
173.     // Cleanup on unmount
174.     useEffect(() => {
175.         return () => {
176.             if (unlistenRef.current) {
177.                 unlistenRef.current();
178.             }
179.             if (updateTimerRef.current) {
180.                 clearTimeout(updateTimerRef.current);
181.             }
182.         };
183.     }, []);
184. 
185.     return {
186.         beacons,
187.         beaconStream,
188.         isCapturing,
189.         error,
190.         startCapture,
191.         stopCapture,
192.     };
193. }
194. 
195. // --- Context Provider to prevent state loss on tab switch ---
196. 
197. const BeaconCaptureContext = createContext<UseBeaconCaptureResult | null>(null);
198. 
199. export function BeaconCaptureProvider({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) {
200.     const captureState = useBeaconCaptureInternal();
201.     return (
202.         <BeaconCaptureContext.Provider value= { captureState } >
203.         { children }
204.         </BeaconCaptureContext.Provider>
205.     );
206. }
207. 
208. export function useBeaconCapture() {
209.     const context = useContext(BeaconCaptureContext);
210.     if (!context) {
211.         throw new Error("useBeaconCapture must be used within a BeaconCaptureProvider");
212.     }
213.     return context;
214. }
215.
````

</details>

1. /**
2.  * Aether — Settings Page
3.  *
4.  * Provides interface management: list adapters, toggle monitor/managed mode,
5.  * and configure the active capture interface.
6.  */
7. 
8. import { useState, useEffect, useCallback } from "react";
9. import { invoke } from "@tauri-apps/api/core";
10. import {
11.     Settings2, RefreshCw, Wifi, WifiOff, ToggleLeft, ToggleRight,
12.     ChevronRight, AlertTriangle, CheckCircle2, Radio
13. } from "lucide-react";
14. import type { NetworkInterface, InterfaceModeResult } from "../types/capture";
15. 
16. export function SettingsPage() {
17.     const [interfaces, setInterfaces] = useState<NetworkInterface[]>([]);
18.     const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);
19.     const [toggleLoading, setToggleLoading] = useState<string | null>(null);
20.     const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);
21.     const [lastResult, setLastResult] = useState<InterfaceModeResult | null>(null);
22. 
23.     const fetchInterfaces = useCallback(async () => {
24.         setLoading(true);
25.         setError(null);
26.         try {
27.             const ifaces = await invoke<NetworkInterface[]>("list_interfaces");
28.             setInterfaces(ifaces);
29.         } catch (err: unknown) {
30.             const msg = typeof err === "string" ? err : JSON.stringify(err);
31.             setError(msg);
32.         } finally {
33.             setLoading(false);
34.         }
35.     }, []);
36. 
37.     useEffect(() => {
38.         fetchInterfaces();
39.     }, [fetchInterfaces]);
40. 
41.     const handleToggleMode = async (ifaceName: string) => {
42.         setToggleLoading(ifaceName);
43.         setError(null);
44.         setLastResult(null);
45. 
46.         try {
47.             const result = await invoke<InterfaceModeResult>("toggle_interface_mode", {
48.                 interfaceName: ifaceName,
49.             });
50.             setLastResult(result);
```

</details>

1. import * as React from "react"
2. import * as TooltipPrimitive from "@radix-ui/react-tooltip"
3. import { clsx, type ClassValue } from "clsx"

5.

7.     return twMerge(clsx(inputs))
8. }
9. 
10. const TooltipProvider = TooltipPrimitive.Provider
11. const Tooltip = TooltipPrimitive.Root
12. const TooltipTrigger = TooltipPrimitive.Trigger
13. 
14. const TooltipContent = React.forwardRef<
15.     React.ElementRef<typeof TooltipPrimitive.Content>,
16.     React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<typeof TooltipPrimitive.Content>
17. >(({ className, sideOffset = 4, ...props }, ref) => (
18.     <TooltipPrimitive.Content
19.         ref={ref}
20.         sideOffset={sideOffset}
21.         className={cn(
22.             "z-50 overflow-hidden rounded-md border border-border bg-popover px-3 py-1.5 text-xs text-popover-foreground shadow-md animate-in fade-in-0 zoom-in-95 data-[state=closed]:animate-out data-[state=closed]:fade-out-0 data-[state=closed]:zoom-out-95 data-[side=bottom]:slide-in-from-top-2 data-[side=left]:slide-in-from-right-2 data-[side=right]:slide-in-from-left-2 data-[side=top]:slide-in-from-bottom-2",
23.             className
24.         )}
25.         {...props}
26.     />
27. ))
28. TooltipContent.displayName = TooltipPrimitive.Content.displayName
29. 
30. export { Tooltip, TooltipTrigger, TooltipContent, TooltipProvider }
31.

**Build Recon scanner page**

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

````json
{
  "agent_type": "general-purpose",
  "description": "Build Recon scanner page",
  "mode": "background",
  "name": "build-recon-page",
  "prompt": "You are building the Recon page for the Aether WiFi Auditor app at /home/kali/Aether_wifi.\n\nThis is a Tauri v2 app with React 19 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS 4 + Lucide icons + Framer Motion + ECharts.\n\n## Design System\nThe app uses a dark cyberpunk/hacker aesthetic:\n- Dark background (hsl(224 71% 4%)), electric cyan primary (#00E5FF)\n- `glass-panel` class = `bg-background/80 backdrop-blur-xl border border-border/50 shadow-2xl`\n- Font: JetBrains Mono for data/headings, Inter for body\n- Radar colors: green (#00FF66), cyan (#00E5FF), yellow (#FFEA00), red (#FF1744)\n- `text-glow` class for glowing text effect\n- Pages use `animate-in fade-in slide-in-from-bottom-4 duration-500` animation\n\n## Reusable Components Available\nImport from `../components/ui/shared`:\n```typescript\nimport { GlassCard, StatCard, StatusBadge, ActionButton, PageHeader, DataTable, TabBar, InputField, SelectField, SignalBar, EmptyState } from \"../components/ui/shared\";\n```\n\nThe `DataTable` component accepts:\n```typescript\ninterface Column<T> {\n  key: string;\n  label: string;\n  align?: \"left\" | \"center\" | \"right\";\n  render?: (item: T) => ReactNode;\n}\n<DataTable columns={columns} data={data} keyField=\"id\" emptyMessage=\"No results\" />\n```\n\n## Available TypeScript Types\nImport from `../types/capture`:\n```typescript\nimport type { HostInfo, PortResult, ServiceInfo } from \"../types/capture\";\n```\n- HostInfo: { ip, mac, hostname, vendor, is_up, timestamp_ms }\n- PortResult: { host, port, protocol, state, service, version }\n- ServiceInfo: { host, port, service, version, mac, vendor }\n\n## Available Tauri Commands\n```typescript\nimport { invoke } from \"@tauri-apps/api/core\";\nconst hosts = await invoke<HostInfo[]>(\"ping_scan\", { subnet: \"192.168.1.0/24\" });\nconst arp = await invoke<HostInfo[]>(\"arp_scan\", { interfaceName: \"eth0\" });\nconst ports = await invoke<PortResult[]>(\"port_scan\", { target: \"192.168.1.1\", ports: \"1-1000\" });\nconst ssh = await invoke<ServiceInfo[]>(\"ssh_scan\", { subnet: \"192.168.1.0/24\" });\nconst telnet = await invoke<ServiceInfo[]>(\"telnet_scan\", { subnet: \"192.168.1.0/24\" });\n```\n\n## Create the file: `src/pages/Recon.tsx`\n\n### Layout:\n1. **PageHeader** with Radar icon, title \"RECON\", subtitle \"NETWORK DISCOVERY\"\n2. **TabBar** with tabs: \"Host Discovery\", \"Port Scan\", \"Services\"\n3. **Content area** that changes based on active tab:\n\n### Tab: Host Discovery\n- Input field for subnet (default \"192.168.1.0/24\")\n- Two action buttons: \"Ping Scan\" and \"ARP Scan\"\n- Stats row: Total Hosts, Up Hosts, Down Hosts\n- DataTable showing results with columns: IP, MAC, Hostname, Vendor, Status\n- Status column should show a green dot for up, red for down\n\n### Tab: Port Scan\n- Input fields: Target IP, Port range (default \"1-1000\")\n- Action button: \"Scan Ports\"\n- DataTable: Host, Port, Protocol, State, Service, Version\n- State column color-coded: open=green, filtered=yellow, closed=red\n\n### Tab: Services\n- Input for subnet\n- Two buttons: \"SSH Scan\" and \"Telnet Scan\"\n- DataTable: Host, Port, Service, Version, MAC, Vendor\n\n### UX Features:\n- Loading spinner on ActionButton while scanning (use `loading` prop)\n- Error display for failed scans\n- Results should persist when switching tabs (useState per tab)\n- Clean, responsive layout matching existing pages\n\n### Important:\n- Do NOT modify any other existing files\n- Export the component as `export function Recon()`\n- Make sure it type-checks with `npx tsc --noEmit`\n- Build with `npm run build`\n"
}
````

</details>

{
  "agent_type": "general-purpose",

  "name": "build-attack-page",
  "prompt": "You are building the Attack page for the Aether WiFi Auditor app at /home/kali/Aether_wifi.\n\nThis is a Tauri v2 app with React 19 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS 4 + Lucide icons + Framer Motion.\n\n## Design System\nDark cyberpunk/hacker aesthetic:\n- Dark background (hsl(224 71% 4%)), electric cyan primary (#00E5FF)\n- `glass-panel` class = `bg-background/80 backdrop-blur-xl border border-border/50 shadow-2xl`\n- Font: JetBrains Mono for data, Inter for body  \n- Radar colors: green (#00FF66), cyan (#00E5FF), yellow (#FFEA00), red (#FF1744)\n- Pages use `animate-in fade-in slide-in-from-bottom-4 duration-500`\n\n## Reusable Components\nImport from `../components/ui/shared`:\n```typescript\nimport { GlassCard, StatCard, StatusBadge, ActionButton, PageHeader, TabBar, InputField, SelectField, EmptyState } from \"../components/ui/shared\";\n```\n\n## Available Tauri Commands (import { invoke } from \"@tauri-apps/api/core\")\n```typescript\n// Beacon spam\nawait invoke(\"start_beacon_spam\", { interfaceName: \"wlan0\", mode: \"list\", ssids: [\"A\",\"B\"] });\nawait invoke(\"start_beacon_spam\", { interfaceName: \"wlan0\", mode: \"random\", count: 50 });\nawait invoke(\"start_beacon_spam\", { interfaceName: \"wlan0\", mode: \"rickroll\" });\nawait invoke(\"stop_attack\");\n\n// Probe flood\nawait invoke(\"start_probe_flood\", { interfaceName: \"wlan0\" });\n\n// Deauth\nawait invoke(\"start_mdk4_deauth\", { interfaceName: \"wlan0\", targetBssid: \"AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF\" }); // targetBssid is optional\n\n// Evil portal\nawait invoke(\"start_bettercap_daemon\", { interfaceName: \"wlan0\" });\nawait invoke(\"stop_bettercap_daemon\");\nawait invoke(\"start_evil_portal\", { ssid: \"FreeWiFi\", channel: 6 });\nawait invoke(\"start_karma_attack\");\nawait invoke(\"bettercap_command\", { command: \"wifi.show\" });\n\n// Advanced attacks\nawait invoke(\"start_channel_switch\", { interfaceName: \"wlan0\", targetBssid: \"AA:BB:CC\", targetChannel: 11 });\nawait invoke(\"start_sleep_attack\", { interfaceName: \"wlan0\", targetBssid: \"AA:BB:CC\" });\nawait invoke(\"start_sae_flood\", { interfaceName: \"wlan0\", targetBssid: \"AA:BB:CC\" });\nawait invoke(\"start_quiet_time\", { interfaceName: \"wlan0\", channel: 6 });\nawait invoke(\"start_bad_message\", { interfaceName: \"wlan0\", targetBssid: \"AA:BB:CC\" });\nawait invoke(\"stop_advanced_attack\");\n```\n\n## Create: `src/pages/Attack.tsx`\n\n### Layout:\n1. **PageHeader**: Zap icon (destructive color), \"ATTACK\", \"OFFENSIVE SUITE\"\n2. **TabBar**: \"Beacon Spam\", \"Deauth\", \"Evil Portal\", \"Advanced\"\n\n### Tab: Beacon Spam\n- GlassCard with radio-button selector for mode:\n  - List (show textarea for SSIDs, one per line)\n  - Random (number input for count, default 50)\n  - RickRoll (no extra inputs, just the button)\n- Interface selector (text input, default \"wlan0\")\n- Big \"LAUNCH\" button (destructive variant) / \"STOP\" button when active\n- StatusBadge showing active/inactive\n\n### Tab: Deauth\n- Target BSSID input (optional — blank = broadcast)\n- Interface input\n- \"Probe Flood\" button and \"Deauth\" button side by side\n- Warning banner: \"⚠️ Deauthentication attacks may be illegal without authorization\"\n\n### Tab: Evil Portal\n- SSID input, channel selector (1-14)\n- \"Start Bettercap\" / \"Stop Bettercap\" toggle\n- \"Launch Evil Portal\" and \"Start Karma\" buttons\n- Bettercap command input with send button (like a mini terminal)\n- Command output display area\n\n### Tab: Advanced\n- Grid of attack cards (2x3), each with:\n  - Icon, name, brief description\n  - Required inputs (target BSSID, channel, etc.)\n  - Launch/Stop button\n  - Attacks: Channel Switch, Sleep Attack, SAE Flood, Quiet Time, Bad Message\n\n### UX:\n- Track which attack is running via state\n- Only one attack can run at a time (per mdk4 limitation)\n- Use destructive red color scheme for attack controls\n- Loading states on buttons\n- Animated warning banners\n\n### Important:\n- Export as `export function Attack()`\n- Do NOT modify any other files\n- Build: `npm run build`\n"
}
````

**Build Sniffer monitor page**

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

````json
{
  "agent_type": "general-purpose",
  "description": "Build Sniffer monitor page",
  "mode": "background",
  "name": "build-sniffer-page",
  "prompt": "You are building the Sniffer page for the Aether WiFi Auditor app at /home/kali/Aether_wifi.\n\nThis is a Tauri v2 app with React 19 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS 4 + Lucide icons + ECharts + Framer Motion.\n\n## Design System\nDark cyberpunk/hacker aesthetic:\n- Dark background (hsl(224 71% 4%)), electric cyan primary (#00E5FF)\n- `glass-panel` class = `bg-background/80 backdrop-blur-xl border border-border/50 shadow-2xl`\n- Font: JetBrains Mono for data, Inter for body\n- Radar colors: green (#00FF66), cyan (#00E5FF), yellow (#FFEA00), red (#FF1744)\n- `text-glow` class for glow effect\n- Pages use `animate-in fade-in slide-in-from-bottom-4 duration-500`\n\n## Reusable Components\n```typescript\nimport { GlassCard, StatCard, StatusBadge, ActionButton, PageHeader, TabBar, InputField, DataTable, EmptyState, SignalBar } from \"../components/ui/shared\";\n```\n\n## Available TypeScript Types (from `../types/capture`)\n```typescript\nexport interface ProbeRequest { source_mac, ssid, rssi, channel, frequency_mhz, vendor, timestamp_ms }\nexport interface DeauthEvent { source_mac, dest_mac, bssid, reason_code, rssi, channel, is_broadcast, vendor, timestamp_ms }\n// Advanced sniffer types are also in capture.ts:\nexport interface PacketStats { channel, total_frames, management_frames, control_frames, data_frames, frames_per_second, timestamp_ms }\nexport interface RawFrame { frame_type, subtype, addr1, addr2, addr3, size, rssi, channel, timestamp_ms }\nexport interface PmkidCapture { bssid, client_mac, pmkid, ssid, hashcat_line, timestamp_ms }\nexport interface PwnagotchiInfo { name, version, uptime, epoch, bssid, channel, rssi, timestamp_ms }\n```\n\n## Available Tauri Commands + Events\n```typescript\nimport { invoke } from \"@tauri-apps/api/core\";\nimport { listen } from \"@tauri-apps/api/event\";\n\n// Basic sniffer (probe requests + deauth detection)\nawait invoke(\"start_sniffer\", { interfaceName: \"wlan0\" });\nawait invoke(\"stop_sniffer\");\n// Events: \"probe-request\" → ProbeRequest, \"deauth-detected\" → DeauthEvent\n\n// Packet monitor\nawait invoke(\"start_packet_monitor\", { interfaceName: \"wlan0\" });\nawait invoke(\"stop_packet_monitor\");\n// Event: \"packet-stats\" → PacketStats\n\n// Raw capture\nawait invoke(\"start_raw_capture\", { interfaceName: \"wlan0\" });\nawait invoke(\"stop_raw_capture\");\n// Event: \"raw-frame\" → RawFrame\n\n// PMKID capture\nawait invoke(\"start_pmkid_capture\", { interfaceName: \"wlan0\" });\nawait invoke(\"stop_pmkid_capture\");\n// Event: \"pmkid-capture\" → PmkidCapture\n\n// Pwnagotchi detection\nawait invoke(\"detect_pwnagotchi\", { interfaceName: \"wlan0\" });\nawait invoke(\"stop_pwnagotchi_detect\");\n// Event: \"pwnagotchi-detected\" → PwnagotchiInfo\n```\n\n## Create: `src/pages/Sniffer.tsx`\n\n### Layout:\n1. **PageHeader**: Eye icon (from lucide), \"SNIFFER\", \"PASSIVE INTELLIGENCE\"\n2. **TabBar**: \"Probe Requests\", \"Deauth Monitor\", \"Packet Stats\", \"PMKID\", \"Pwnagotchi\"\n\n### Tab: Probe Requests\n- Start/Stop sniffer button\n- StatusBadge showing active/inactive\n- Real-time scrolling log of probe requests (listen to \"probe-request\" events)\n- DataTable columns: Time, Source MAC, SSID, Channel, RSSI (with SignalBar), Vendor\n- Stats at top: Total Probes, Unique Devices, Unique SSIDs (calculated from data)\n- Time should display as HH:MM:SS from timestamp_ms\n- Keep last 500 entries (ring buffer in state)\n\n### Tab: Deauth Monitor\n- Same start/stop sniffer button\n- Real-time log of deauth/disassociation events\n- DataTable: Time, Source, Target, BSSID, Reason Code, Broadcast?, RSSI\n- Broadcast deauths highlighted in red\n- Alert counter at top showing total deauth events detected\n- Visual alert indicator when new deauths detected (flashing red border)\n\n### Tab: Packet Stats\n- Start/Stop packet monitor\n- Real-time frame count display using ECharts:\n  - Donut chart showing frame type distribution (management/control/data)\n  - Line chart showing frames-per-second over time (last 60 seconds)\n- Stat cards: Total Frames, Management, Control, Data, FPS\n\n### Tab: PMKID\n- Start/Stop PMKID capture button\n- DataTable: Time, BSSID, SSID, Client MAC, PMKID (truncated)\n- \"Copy Hashcat Line\" button on each row\n- Export all as .22000 file button\n\n### Tab: Pwnagotchi\n- Start/Stop detection button\n- When a pwnagotchi is found, show a card with:\n  - Name, version, uptime, epoch count\n  - BSSID, channel, RSSI\n  - Fun styling with a ghost/robot icon\n\n### UX:\n- Events should auto-scroll to latest entry\n- Each tab maintains its own state independently\n- Use React refs for auto-scroll behavior\n- Format timestamps as readable time (HH:MM:SS)\n\n### Important:\n- Export as `export function Sniffer()`\n- Do NOT modify any other files\n- Build with `npm run build` to verify\n"
}
````

Motion.\n\n## Design System\nDark cyberpunk/hacker aesthetic:\n- Dark background (hsl(224 71% 4%)), electric cyan primary (#00E5FF)\n- `glass-panel` class = `bg-background/80 backdrop-blur-xl border border-border/50 shadow-2xl`\n- Font: JetBrains Mono for data, Inter for body\n- Radar colors: green (#00FF66), cyan (#00E5FF), yellow (#FFEA00), red (#FF1744)\n- Pages use `animate-in fade-in slide-in-from-bottom-4 duration-500`\n\n## Reusable Components\n```typescript\nimport { GlassCard, StatCard, StatusBadge, ActionButton, PageHeader, TabBar, InputField, DataTable, EmptyState } from \"../components/ui/shared\";\n```\n\n## Available Types (from `../types/capture`)\n```typescript\nexport interface MacSpoofResult { interface_name, original_mac, new_mac, vendor, success, message }\nexport interface SsidList { name, ssids: string[] }\nexport interface SavedAp { bssid, ssid, channel, rssi, encryption, vendor, first_seen_ms, last_seen_ms, client_count, selected }\nexport interface WardriveEntry { bssid, ssid, channel, rssi, encryption, vendor, latitude, longitude, altitude, timestamp_ms }\nexport interface GpsLocation { latitude, longitude, altitude, speed, timestamp, fix_mode }\n```\n\n## Available Tauri Commands\n```typescript\nimport { invoke } from \"@tauri-apps/api/core\";\n\n// MAC Spoof\nconst result = await invoke<MacSpoofResult>(\"spoof_mac\", { interfaceName: \"wlan0\", newMac: null }); // null = random\nconst result = await invoke<MacSpoofResult>(\"restore_mac\", { interfaceName: \"wlan0\" });\n\n// SSID Manager\nconst names = await invoke<string[]>(\"list_ssid_lists\");\nconst list = await invoke<SsidList | null>(\"get_ssid_list\", { name: \"my_list\" });\nawait invoke(\"save_ssid_list\", { name: \"my_list\", ssids: [\"SSID1\", \"SSID2\"] });\nawait invoke(\"delete_ssid_list\", { name: \"my_list\" });\nconst generated = await invoke<string[]>(\"generate_random_ssids\", { count: 20, maxLen: 16 });\n\n// AP Manager\nconst aps = await invoke<SavedAp[]>(\"load_saved_aps\");\nawait invoke(\"save_scan_aps\", { aps: [...] });\nawait invoke(\"select_target_aps\", { bssids: [\"AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF\"], selected: true });\nconst selected = await invoke<SavedAp[]>(\"get_selected_aps\");\nawait invoke(\"clear_saved_aps\");\n\n// WiFi Join\nawait invoke<string>(\"join_wifi\", { interfaceName: \"wlan0\", ssid: \"MyNetwork\", password: \"pass123\" });\nawait invoke<string>(\"disconnect_wifi\", { interfaceName: \"wlan0\" });\n\n// Wardrive\nconst gps = await invoke<GpsLocation | null>(\"get_gps_location\");\nawait invoke<number>(\"export_wardrive_csv\", { entries: [...], outputPath: \"/tmp/wardrive.csv\" });\nawait invoke<number>(\"export_wardrive_kml\", { entries: [...], outputPath: \"/tmp/wardrive.kml\" });\n```\n\n## Create: `src/pages/Tools.tsx`\n\n### Layout:\n1. **PageHeader**: Wrench icon, \"TOOLS\", \"UTILITY ARSENAL\"\n2. **TabBar**: \"MAC Spoof\", \"SSID Manager\", \"AP Manager\", \"WiFi\", \"Wardrive\"\n\n### Tab: MAC Spoof\n- Interface name input (default \"wlan0\")\n- Optional new MAC input (empty = random)\n- Two buttons: \"Randomize MAC\" (primary) and \"Restore Original\" (ghost)\n- Result display showing: Original MAC → New MAC, Vendor\n- Success/error status\n\n### Tab: SSID Manager\n- Left panel: list of saved SSID lists (clickable to select)\n- Right panel: edit area showing SSIDs in the selected list\n  - Textarea with SSIDs (one per line)\n  - Save / Delete buttons\n  - Generate Random button with count input\n- New list creation (name input + create button)\n\n### Tab: AP Manager\n- DataTable showing all saved APs with checkbox column for selection\n- Columns: Selected (checkbox), BSSID, SSID, Channel, RSSI, Encryption, Vendor\n- Toolbar: \"Save from Scan\" button, \"Clear All\" button\n- Selected count display\n- Integration note: selected APs are used as targets in Attack page\n\n### Tab: WiFi\n- Interface name input\n- SSID input, password input\n- \"Connect\" button, \"Disconnect\" button\n- Status display showing connection result\n\n### Tab: Wardrive  \n- GPS status indicator (with fix mode)\n- \"Check GPS\" button that calls get_gps_location\n- Lat/Lon/Alt display when GPS available\n- Export buttons: \"Export WiGLE CSV\" and \"Export KML\"\n- Note when no GPS: \"GPS not available. Install gpsd for location data.\"\n\n### UX:\n- All tabs maintain state independently\n- Loading states on buttons during async operations\n- Error/success toasts using styled divs\n- Clean form layouts with consistent spacing\n\n### Important:\n- Export as `export function Tools()`\n- Do NOT modify any other files\n- Build: `npm run build`\n"
}
````

<sub>⏱️ 224m 36s</sub>

### ℹ️ Notification

Background agent "Build Attack control page" (general-purpose) completed

<details>
<summary>Detail</summary>

You are building the Attack page for the Aether WiFi Auditor app at /home/kali/Aether_wifi.

This is a Tauri v2 app with React 19 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS 4 + Lucide icons + Framer Motion.

## Design System
Dark cyberpunk/hacker aesthetic:
- Dark background (hsl(224 71% 4%)), electric cyan primary (#00E5FF)
- `glass-panel` class = `bg-background/80 backdrop-blur-xl border border-border/50 shadow-2xl`
- Font: JetBrains Mono for data, Inter for body  
- Radar colors: green (#00FF66), cyan (#00E5FF), yellow (#FFEA00), red (#FF1744)
- Pages use `animate-in fade-in slide-in-from-bottom-4 duration-500`

## Reusable Components
Import from `../components/ui/shared`:
```typescript
import { GlassCard, StatCard, StatusBadge, ActionButton, PageHeader, TabBar, InputField, SelectField, EmptyState } from "../components/ui/shared";
```

## Available Tauri Commands (import { invoke } from "@tauri-apps/api/core")
```typescript
// Beacon spam
await invoke("start_beacon_spam", { interfaceName: "wlan0", mode: "list", ssids: ["A","B"] });
await invoke("start_beacon_spam", { interfaceName: "wlan0", mode: "random", count: 50 });
await invoke("start_beacon_spam", { interfaceName: "wlan0", mode: "rickroll" });
await invoke("stop_attack");

// Probe flood
await invoke("start_probe_flood", { interfaceName: "wlan0" });

// Deauth
await invoke("start_mdk4_deauth", { interfaceName: "wlan0", targetBssid: "AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF" }); // targetBssid is optional

// Evil portal
await invoke("start_bettercap_daemon", { interfaceName: "wlan0" });
await invoke("stop_bettercap_daemon");
await invoke("start_evil_portal", { ssid: "FreeWiFi", channel: 6 });
await invoke("start_karma_attack");
await invoke("bettercap_command", { command: "wifi.show" });

// Advanced attacks
await invoke("start_channel_switch", { interfaceName: "wlan0", targetBssid: "AA:BB:CC", targetChannel: 11 });
await invoke("start_sleep_attack", { interfaceName: "wlan0", targetBssid: "AA:BB:CC" });
await invoke("start_sae_flood", { interfaceName: "wlan0", targetBssid: "AA:BB:CC" });
await invoke("start_quiet_time", { interfaceName: "wlan0", channel: 6 });
await invoke("start_bad_message", { interfaceName: "wlan0", targetBssid: "AA:BB:CC" });
await invoke("stop_advanced_attack");
```

## Create: `src/pages/Attack.tsx`

### Layout:
1. **PageHeader**: Zap icon (destructive color), "ATTACK", "OFFENSIVE SUITE"
2. **TabBar**: "Beacon Spam", "Deauth", "Evil Portal", "Advanced"

### Tab: Beacon Spam
- GlassCard with radio-button selector for mode:
  - List (show textarea for SSIDs, one per line)
  - Random (number input for count, default 50)
  - RickRoll (no extra inputs, just the button)
- Interface selector (text input, default "wlan0")
- Big "LAUNCH" button (destructive variant) / "STOP" button when active
- StatusBadge showing active/inactive

### Tab: Deauth
- Target BSSID input (optional — blank = broadcast)
- Interface input
- "Probe Flood" button and "Deauth" button side by side
- Warning banner: "⚠️ Deauthentication attacks may be illegal without authorization"

### Tab: Evil Portal
- SSID input, channel selector (1-14)
- "Start Bettercap" / "Stop Bettercap" toggle
- "Launch Evil Portal" and "Start Karma" buttons
- Bettercap command input with send button (like a mini terminal)
- Command output display area

### Tab: Advanced
- Grid of attack cards (2x3), each with:
  - Icon, name, brief description
  - Required inputs (target BSSID, channel, etc.)
  - Launch/Stop button
  - Attacks: Channel Switch, Sleep Attack, SAE Flood, Quiet Time, Bad Message

### UX:
- Track which attack is running via state
- Only one attack can run at a time (per mdk4 limitation)
- Use destructive red color scheme for attack controls
- Loading states on buttons
- Animated warning banners

### Important:
- Export as `export function Attack()`
- Do NOT modify any other files
- Build: `npm run build`


</details>

<details>
<summary>Detail</summary>

You are building the Tools page for the Aether WiFi Auditor app at /home/kali/Aether_wifi.

This is a Tauri v2 app with React 19 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS 4 + Lucide icons + Framer Motion.

## Design System
Dark cyberpunk/hacker aesthetic:
- Dark background (hsl(224 71% 4%)), electric cyan primary (#00E5FF)
- `glass-panel` class = `bg-background/80 backdrop-blur-xl border border-border/50 shadow-2xl`
- Font: JetBrains Mono for data, Inter for body
- Radar colors: green (#00FF66), cyan (#00E5FF), yellow (#FFEA00), red (#FF1744)
- Pages use `animate-in fade-in slide-in-from-bottom-4 duration-500`

## Reusable Components
```typescript
import { GlassCard, StatCard, StatusBadge, ActionButton, PageHeader, TabBar, InputField, DataTable, EmptyState } from "../components/ui/shared";
```

## Available Types (from `../types/capture`)
```typescript
export interface MacSpoofResult { interface_name, original_mac, new_mac, vendor, success, message }
export interface SsidList { name, ssids: string[] }
export interface SavedAp { bssid, ssid, channel, rssi, encryption, vendor, first_seen_ms, last_seen_ms, client_count, selected }
export interface WardriveEntry { bssid, ssid, channel, rssi, encryption, vendor, latitude, longitude, altitude, timestamp_ms }
export interface GpsLocation { latitude, longitude, altitude, speed, timestamp, fix_mode }
```

## Available Tauri Commands
```typescript
import { invoke } from "@tauri-apps/api/core";

// MAC Spoof
const result = await invoke<MacSpoofResult>("spoof_mac", { interfaceName: "wlan0", newMac: null }); // null = random
const result = await invoke<MacSpoofResult>("restore_mac", { interfaceName: "wlan0" });

// SSID Manager
const names = await invoke<string[]>("list_ssid_lists");
const list = await invoke<SsidList | null>("get_ssid_list", { name: "my_list" });
await invoke("save_ssid_list", { name: "my_list", ssids: ["SSID1", "SSID2"] });
await invoke("delete_ssid_list", { name: "my_list" });
const generated = await invoke<string[]>("generate_random_ssids", { count: 20, maxLen: 16 });

// AP Manager
const aps = await invoke<SavedAp[]>("load_saved_aps");
await invoke("save_scan_aps", { aps: [...] });
await invoke("select_target_aps", { bssids: ["AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF"], selected: true });
const selected = await invoke<SavedAp[]>("get_selected_aps");
await invoke("clear_saved_aps");

// WiFi Join
await invoke<string>("join_wifi", { interfaceName: "wlan0", ssid: "MyNetwork", password: "pass123" });
await invoke<string>("disconnect_wifi", { interfaceName: "wlan0" });

// Wardrive
const gps = await invoke<GpsLocation | null>("get_gps_location");
await invoke<number>("export_wardrive_csv", { entries: [...], outputPath: "/tmp/wardrive.csv" });
await invoke<number>("export_wardrive_kml", { entries: [...], outputPath: "/tmp/wardrive.kml" });
```

## Create: `src/pages/Tools.tsx`

### Layout:
1. **PageHeader**: Wrench icon, "TOOLS", "UTILITY ARSENAL"
2. **TabBar**: "MAC Spoof", "SSID Manager", "AP Manager", "WiFi", "Wardrive"

### Tab: MAC Spoof
- Interface name input (default "wlan0")
- Optional new MAC input (empty = random)
- Two buttons: "Randomize MAC" (primary) and "Restore Original" (ghost)
- Result display showing: Original MAC → New MAC, Vendor
- Success/error status

### Tab: SSID Manager
- Left panel: list of saved SSID lists (clickable to select)
- Right panel: edit area showing SSIDs in the selected list
  - Textarea with SSIDs (one per line)
  - Save / Delete buttons
  - Generate Random button with count input
- New list creation (name input + create button)

### Tab: AP Manager
- DataTable showing all saved APs with checkbox column for selection
- Columns: Selected (checkbox), BSSID, SSID, Channel, RSSI, Encryption, Vendor
- Toolbar: "Save from Scan" button, "Clear All" button
- Selected count display
- Integration note: selected APs are used as targets in Attack page

### Tab: WiFi
- Interface name input
- SSID input, password input
- "Connect" button, "Disconnect" button
- Status display showing connection result

### Tab: Wardrive  
- GPS status indicator (with fix mode)
- "Check GPS" button that calls get_gps_location
- Lat/Lon/Alt display when GPS available
- Export buttons: "Export WiGLE CSV" and "Export KML"
- Note when no GPS: "GPS not available. Install gpsd for location data."

### UX:
- All tabs maintain state independently
- Loading states on buttons during async operations
- Error/success toasts using styled divs
- Clean form layouts with consistent spacing

### Important:
- Export as `export function Tools()`
- Do NOT modify any other files
- Build: `npm run build`


</details>

### ℹ️ Notification

Background agent "Build Recon scanner page" (general-purpose) completed

<details>
<summary>Detail</summary>

You are building the Recon page for the Aether WiFi Auditor app at /home/kali/Aether_wifi.

This is a Tauri v2 app with React 19 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS 4 + Lucide icons + Framer Motion + ECharts.

## Design System
The app uses a dark cyberpunk/hacker aesthetic:
- Dark background (hsl(224 71% 4%)), electric cyan primary (#00E5FF)
- `glass-panel` class = `bg-background/80 backdrop-blur-xl border border-border/50 shadow-2xl`
- Font: JetBrains Mono for data/headings, Inter for body
- Radar colors: green (#00FF66), cyan (#00E5FF), yellow (#FFEA00), red (#FF1744)
- `text-glow` class for glowing text effect
- Pages use `animate-in fade-in slide-in-from-bottom-4 duration-500` animation

## Reusable Components Available
Import from `../components/ui/shared`:
```typescript
import { GlassCard, StatCard, StatusBadge, ActionButton, PageHeader, DataTable, TabBar, InputField, SelectField, SignalBar, EmptyState } from "../components/ui/shared";
```

The `DataTable` component accepts:
```typescript
interface Column<T> {
  key: string;
  label: string;
  align?: "left" | "center" | "right";
  render?: (item: T) => ReactNode;
}
<DataTable columns={columns} data={data} keyField="id" emptyMessage="No results" />
```

## Available TypeScript Types
Import from `../types/capture`:
```typescript
import type { HostInfo, PortResult, ServiceInfo } from "../types/capture";
```
- HostInfo: { ip, mac, hostname, vendor, is_up, timestamp_ms }
- PortResult: { host, port, protocol, state, service, version }
- ServiceInfo: { host, port, service, version, mac, vendor }

## Available Tauri Commands
```typescript
import { invoke } from "@tauri-apps/api/core";
const hosts = await invoke<HostInfo[]>("ping_scan", { subnet: "192.168.1.0/24" });
const arp = await invoke<HostInfo[]>("arp_scan", { interfaceName: "eth0" });
const ports = await invoke<PortResult[]>("port_scan", { target: "192.168.1.1", ports: "1-1000" });
const ssh = await invoke<ServiceInfo[]>("ssh_scan", { subnet: "192.168.1.0/24" });
const telnet = await invoke<ServiceInfo[]>("telnet_scan", { subnet: "192.168.1.0/24" });
```

## Create the file: `src/pages/Recon.tsx`

### Layout:
1. **PageHeader** with Radar icon, title "RECON", subtitle "NETWORK DISCOVERY"
2. **TabBar** with tabs: "Host Discovery", "Port Scan", "Services"
3. **Content area** that changes based on active tab:

### Tab: Host Discovery
- Input field for subnet (default "192.168.1.0/24")
- Two action buttons: "Ping Scan" and "ARP Scan"
- Stats row: Total Hosts, Up Hosts, Down Hosts
- DataTable showing results with columns: IP, MAC, Hostname, Vendor, Status
- Status column should show a green dot for up, red for down

### Tab: Port Scan
- Input fields: Target IP, Port range (default "1-1000")
- Action button: "Scan Ports"
- DataTable: Host, Port, Protocol, State, Service, Version
- State column color-coded: open=green, filtered=yellow, closed=red

### Tab: Services
- Input for subnet
- Two buttons: "SSH Scan" and "Telnet Scan"
- DataTable: Host, Port, Service, Version, MAC, Vendor

### UX Features:
- Loading spinner on ActionButton while scanning (use `loading` prop)
- Error display for failed scans
- Results should persist when switching tabs (useState per tab)
- Clean, responsive layout matching existing pages

### Important:
- Do NOT modify any other existing files
- Export the component as `export function Recon()`
- Make sure it type-checks with `npx tsc --noEmit`
- Build with `npm run build`


</details>

## Design System
Dark cyberpunk/hacker aesthetic:
- Dark background (hsl(224 71% 4%)), electric cyan primary (#00E5FF)
- `glass-panel` class = `bg-background/80 backdrop-blur-xl border border-border/50 shadow-2xl`
- Font: JetBrains Mono for data, Inter for body
- Radar colors: green (#00FF66), cyan (#00E5FF), yellow (#FFEA00), red (#FF1744)
- `text-glow` class for glow effect
- Pages use `animate-in fade-in slide-in-from-bottom-4 duration-500`

## Reusable Components
```typescript
import { GlassCard, StatCard, StatusBadge, ActionButton, PageHeader, TabBar, InputField, DataTable, EmptyState, SignalBar } from "../components/ui/shared";
```

## Available TypeScript Types (from `../types/capture`)
```typescript
export interface ProbeRequest { source_mac, ssid, rssi, channel, frequency_mhz, vendor, timestamp_ms }
export interface DeauthEvent { source_mac, dest_mac, bssid, reason_code, rssi, channel, is_broadcast, vendor, timestamp_ms }
// Advanced sniffer types are also in capture.ts:
export interface PacketStats { channel, total_frames, management_frames, control_frames, data_frames, frames_per_second, timestamp_ms }
export interface RawFrame { frame_type, subtype, addr1, addr2, addr3, size, rssi, channel, timestamp_ms }
export interface PmkidCapture { bssid, client_mac, pmkid, ssid, hashcat_line, timestamp_ms }
export interface PwnagotchiInfo { name, version, uptime, epoch, bssid, channel, rssi, timestamp_ms }
```

## Available Tauri Commands + Events
```typescript
import { invoke } from "@tauri-apps/api/core";
import { listen } from "@tauri-apps/api/event";

// Basic sniffer (probe requests + deauth detection)
await invoke("start_sniffer", { interfaceName: "wlan0" });
await invoke("stop_sniffer");
// Events: "probe-request" → ProbeRequest, "deauth-detected" → DeauthEvent

// Packet monitor
await invoke("start_packet_monitor", { interfaceName: "wlan0" });
await invoke("stop_packet_monitor");
// Event: "packet-stats" → PacketStats

// Raw capture
await invoke("start_raw_capture", { interfaceName: "wlan0" });
await invoke("stop_raw_capture");
// Event: "raw-frame" → RawFrame

// PMKID capture
await invoke("start_pmkid_capture", { interfaceName: "wlan0" });
await invoke("stop_pmkid_capture");
// Event: "pmkid-capture" → PmkidCapture

// Pwnagotchi detection
await invoke("detect_pwnagotchi", { interfaceName: "wlan0" });
await invoke("stop_pwnagotchi_detect");
// Event: "pwnagotchi-detected" → PwnagotchiInfo
```

## Create: `src/pages/Sniffer.tsx`

### Layout:
1. **PageHeader**: Eye icon (from lucide), "SNIFFER", "PASSIVE INTELLIGENCE"
2. **TabBar**: "Probe Requests", "Deauth Monitor", "Packet Stats", "PMKID", "Pwnagotchi"

### Tab: Probe Requests
- Start/Stop sniffer button
- StatusBadge showing active/inactive
- Real-time scrolling log of probe requests (listen to "probe-request" events)
- DataTable columns: Time, Source MAC, SSID, Channel, RSSI (with SignalBar), Vendor
- Stats at top: Total Probes, Unique Devices, Unique SSIDs (calculated from data)
- Time should display as HH:MM:SS from timestamp_ms
- Keep last 500 entries (ring buffer in state)

### Tab: Deauth Monitor
- Same start/stop sniffer button
- Real-time log of deauth/disassociation events
- DataTable: Time, Source, Target, BSSID, Reason Code, Broadcast?, RSSI
- Broadcast deauths highlighted in red
- Alert counter at top showing total deauth events detected
- Visual alert indicator when new deauths detected (flashing red border)

### Tab: Packet Stats
- Start/Stop packet monitor
- Real-time frame count display using ECharts:
  - Donut chart showing frame type distribution (management/control/data)
  - Line chart showing frames-per-second over time (last 60 seconds)
- Stat cards: Total Frames, Management, Control, Data, FPS

### Tab: PMKID
- Start/Stop PMKID capture button
- DataTable: Time, BSSID, SSID, Client MAC, PMKID (truncated)
- "Copy Hashcat Line" button on each row
- Export all as .22000 file button

### Tab: Pwnagotchi
- Start/Stop detection button
- When a pwnagotchi is found, show a card with:
  - Name, version, uptime, epoch count
  - BSSID, channel, RSSI
  - Fun styling with a ghost/robot icon

### UX:
- Events should auto-scroll to latest entry
- Each tab maintains its own state independently
- Use React refs for auto-scroll behavior
- Format timestamps as readable time (HH:MM:SS)

### Important:
- Export as `export function Sniffer()`
- Do NOT modify any other files
- Build with `npm run build` to verify


</details>

@import "tailwindcss";
@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@400;500;600;700&family=JetBrains+Mono:wght@400;500;700&display=swap');
@import "tw-animate-css";
@import "shadcn/tailwind.css";

@custom-variant dark (&:is(.dark *));

@theme inline {
  --font-sans: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif;
  --font-mono: 'JetBrains Mono', 'Fira Code', monospace;

  --color-sidebar-ring: var(--sidebar-ring);
  --color-sidebar-border: var(--sidebar-border);
  --color-sidebar-accent-foreground: var(--sidebar-accent-foreground);
  --color-sidebar-accent: var(--sidebar-accent);
  --color-sidebar-primary-foreground: var(--sidebar-primary-foreground);
  --color-sidebar-primary: var(--sidebar-primary);
  --color-sidebar-foreground: var(--sidebar-foreground);
  --color-sidebar: var(--sidebar);
  --color-chart-5: var(--chart-5);
  --color-chart-4: var(--chart-4);
  --color-chart-3: var(--chart-3);
  --color-chart-2: var(--chart-2);
  --color-chart-1: var(--chart-1);
  --color-ring: var(--ring);
  --color-input: var(--input);
  --color-border: var(--border);
  --color-destructive: var(--destructive);
  --color-accent-foreground: var(--accent-foreground);
  --color-accent: var(--accent);
  --color-muted-foreground: var(--muted-foreground);
  --color-muted: var(--muted);
  --color-secondary-foreground: var(--secondary-foreground);
  --color-secondary: var(--secondary);
  --color-primary-foreground: var(--primary-foreground);
  --color-primary: var(--primary);
  --color-popover-foreground: var(--popover-foreground);
  --color-popover: var(--popover);
  --color-card-foreground: var(--card-foreground);
  --color-card: var(--card);
  --color-foreground: var(--foreground);
  --color-background: var(--background);
  --radius-sm: calc(var(--radius) * 0.6);
  --radius-md: calc(var(--radius) * 0.8);
  --radius-lg: var(--radius);
  --radius-xl: calc(var(--radius) * 1.4);
  --radius-2xl: calc(var(--radius) * 1.8);
  --radius-3xl: calc(var(--radius) * 2.2);
  --radius-4xl: calc(var(--radius) * 2.6);

  /* Aether signal colors */
  --color-radar-green: #00FF66;
  --color-radar-cyan: #00E5FF;
  --color-radar-yellow: #FFEA00;
  --color-radar-red: #FF1744;
}

:root {
  --background: oklch(0.145 0 0);
  --foreground: oklch(0.985 0 0);
  --card: oklch(0.205 0 0);
  --card-foreground: oklch(0.985 0 0);
  --popover: oklch(0.205 0 0);
  --popover-foreground: oklch(0.985 0 0);
  --primary: oklch(0.75 0.15 195);
  --primary-foreground: oklch(0.145 0 0);
  --secondary: oklch(0.269 0 0);
  --secondary-foreground: oklch(0.985 0 0);
  --muted: oklch(0.269 0 0);
  --muted-foreground: oklch(0.708 0 0);
  --accent: oklch(0.269 0 0);
  --accent-foreground: oklch(0.985 0 0);
  --destructive: oklch(0.577 0.245 27.325);
  --border: oklch(1 0 0 / 10%);
  --input: oklch(1 0 0 / 15%);
  --ring: oklch(0.75 0.15 195);
  --chart-1: oklch(0.75 0.15 195);
  --chart-2: oklch(0.7 0.2 150);
  --chart-3: oklch(0.7 0.2 60);
  --chart-4: oklch(0.6 0.25 30);
  --chart-5: oklch(0.5 0.15 270);
  --radius: 0.625rem;
  --sidebar: oklch(0.205 0 0);
  --sidebar-foreground: oklch(0.985 0 0);
  --sidebar-primary: oklch(0.75 0.15 195);
  --sidebar-primary-foreground: oklch(0.985 0 0);
  --sidebar-accent: oklch(0.269 0 0);
  --sidebar-accent-foreground: oklch(0.985 0 0);
  --sidebar-border: oklch(1 0 0 / 10%);
  --sidebar-ring: oklch(0.556 0 0);
}

@layer base {
  body {
    @apply bg-background text-foreground font-sans antialiased overflow-hidden;
  }
  * {
    @apply border-border outline-ring/50;
  }
}

@layer components {
  .text-glow {
    text-shadow: 0 0 10px oklch(0.75 0.15 195 / 0.5);
  }
}

1. import { Activity, Target, Shield, LayoutDashboard, Terminal, Stethoscope, Settings2 } from "lucide-react";
2. import { Link, useLocation } from "react-router-dom";
3. import { Tooltip, TooltipContent, TooltipProvider, TooltipTrigger } from "../ui/tooltip";
4. import { cn } from "../ui/tooltip";
5. 
6. const NAV_ITEMS = [
7.     { id: "dashboard", icon: LayoutDashboard, label: "Dashboard", path: "/" },
8.     { id: "spectrum", icon: Activity, label: "Spectrum", path: "/spectrum" },
9.     { id: "hunt", icon: Target, label: "Hunt Mode", path: "/hunt" },
10.     { id: "audit", icon: Shield, label: "Audit Suite", path: "/audit" },
11.     { id: "doctor", icon: Stethoscope, label: "Env Doctor", path: "/doctor" },
12.     { id: "settings", icon: Settings2, label: "Settings", path: "/settings" },
13. ];
14. 
15. export function Sidebar() {
16.     const location = useLocation();
17. 
18.     return (
19.         <aside className="w-16 h-screen flex flex-col items-center py-6 glass-panel border-r border-border/50 bg-background/95 shrink-0 z-50 overflow-hidden relative">
20.             {/* Decorative top accent */}
21.             <div className="absolute top-0 left-0 w-full h-[2px] bg-primary/40 shadow-glow-primary"></div>
22. 
23.             <div className="mb-10 text-primary">
24.                 <Terminal className="w-7 h-7" strokeWidth={1.5} />
25.             </div>
26. 
27.             <nav className="flex-1 flex flex-col gap-3 w-full px-2">
28.                 <TooltipProvider delayDuration={0}>
29.                     {NAV_ITEMS.map((item) => {
30.                         const isActive = location.pathname === item.path;
31.                         const Icon = item.icon;
32. 
33.                         return (
34.                             <Tooltip key={item.id}>
35.                                 <TooltipTrigger asChild>
36.                                     <Link
37.                                         to={item.path}
38.                                         className={cn(
39.                                             "w-12 h-12 flex flex-col items-center justify-center rounded-lg transition-all duration-300 group relative",
40.                                             isActive
41.                                                 ? "bg-primary/10 text-primary shadow-[inset_0_0_10px_rgba(0,229,255,0.1)]"
42.                                                 : "text-muted-foreground hover:bg-muted/50 hover:text-foreground"
43.                                         )}
44.                                     >
45.                                         {isActive && (
46.                                             <div className="absolute left-0 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 w-1 h-6 bg-primary rounded-r-full shadow-glow-primary"></div>
47.                                         )}
48.                                         <Icon className="w-5 h-5 transition-transform group-hover:scale-110" strokeWidth={isActive ? 2 : 1.5} />
49.                                     </Link>
50.                                 </TooltipTrigger>
51.                                 <TooltipContent side="right" sideOffset={10} className="font-mono text-[10px] uppercase tracking-wider bg-card text-foreground border-border/60">
52.                                     {item.label}
53.                                 </TooltipContent>
54.                             </Tooltip>
55.                         );
56.                     })}
57.                 </TooltipProvider>
58.             </nav>
59. 
60.             <div className="mt-auto px-2">
61.                 <div className="w-10 h-10 rounded-full bg-secondary border border-border/50 flex items-center justify-center">
62.                     <div className="w-2 h-2 rounded-full bg-radar-green shadow-glow-radar animate-pulse-fast"></div>
63.                 </div>
64.             </div>
65.         </aside>
66.     );
67. }
68.

````
1. /**
2.  * Aether — useBeaconCapture Hook
3.  *
4.  * A React hook that manages the lifecycle of a packet capture session,
5.  * providing a real-time stream of parsed 802.11 Beacon frames.
6.  *
7.  * @example
8.  * ```tsx
9.  * function SpectrumView() {
10.  *   const { beacons, isCapturing, error, startCapture, stopCapture } =
11.  *     useBeaconCapture();
12.  *
13.  *   return (
14.  *     <div>
15.  *       <button onClick={() => startCapture('wlan0mon')}>Start</button>
16.  *       <button onClick={stopCapture}>Stop</button>
17.  *       {isCapturing && <span>Capturing...</span>}
18.  *       {error && <span>Error: {error}</span>}
19.  *       <ul>
20.  *         {Array.from(beacons.values()).map((b) => (
21.  *           <li key={b.bssid}>
22.  *             {b.ssid || '(hidden)'} — CH{b.channel} — {b.rssi}dBm
23.  *           </li>
24.  *         ))}
25.  *       </ul>
26.  *     </div>
27.  *   );
28.  * }
29.  * ```
30.  */
31. 
32. import { useCallback, useEffect, useRef, useState, createContext, useContext, ReactNode } from 'react';
33. import { invoke } from '@tauri-apps/api/core';
34. import { listen, type UnlistenFn } from '@tauri-apps/api/event';
35. import type { BeaconFrame, CaptureStatus } from '../types/capture';
36. 
37. /**
38.  * Return type of the `useBeaconCapture` hook.
39.  */
40. export interface UseBeaconCaptureResult {
41.     /** Map of BSSID → latest BeaconFrame (deduped, most recent per AP) */
42.     beacons: Map<string, BeaconFrame>;
43. 
44.     /** Ordered list of all beacon events received (for time-series views) */
45.     beaconStream: BeaconFrame[];
46. 
47.     /** Whether a capture is currently active */
48.     isCapturing: boolean;
49. 
50.     /** Error message from the last failed operation, or null */
51.     error: string | null;
52. 
53.     /** Start capturing on the given interface */
54.     startCapture: (interfaceName: string) => Promise<void>;
55. 
56.     /** Stop the current capture session */
57.     stopCapture: () => Promise<void>;
58. }
59. 
60. /**
61.  * Hook for managing real-time 802.11 beacon capture.
62.  *
63.  * - Deduplicates beacons by BSSID (keeps latest reading)
64.  * - Provides both a deduplicated map and a raw stream array
65.  * - Auto-cleans up the Tauri event listener on unmount
66.  *
67.  * @param maxStreamLength Max entries to keep in `beaconStream` (default: 1000)
68.  */
69. function useBeaconCaptureInternal(maxStreamLength = 1000): UseBeaconCaptureResult {
70.     const [beacons, setBeacons] = useState<Map<string, BeaconFrame>>(new Map());
71.     const [beaconStream, setBeaconStream] = useState<BeaconFrame[]>([]);
72.     const [isCapturing, setIsCapturing] = useState(false);
73.     const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);
74. 
75.     // Refs for the cleanup function
76.     const unlistenRef = useRef<UnlistenFn | null>(null);
77.     const streamRef = useRef<BeaconFrame[]>([]);
78.     const beaconsRef = useRef<Map<string, BeaconFrame>>(new Map());
79. 
80.     // Throttle state updates to avoid excessive re-renders
81.     const updateTimerRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null>(null);
82.     const pendingRef = useRef(false);
83. 
84.     const flushUpdates = useCallback(() => {
85.         setBeacons(new Map(beaconsRef.current));
86.         setBeaconStream([...streamRef.current]);
87.         pendingRef.current = false;
88.         updateTimerRef.current = null;
89.     }, []);
90. 
91.     const scheduleUpdate = useCallback(() => {
92.         if (!pendingRef.current) {
93.             pendingRef.current = true;
94.             // Batch updates every 250ms to keep the UI smooth at 60fps
95.             updateTimerRef.current = setTimeout(flushUpdates, 250);
96.         }
97.     }, [flushUpdates]);
98. 
99.     const startCapture = useCallback(
100.         async (interfaceName: string) => {
101.             try {
102.                 setError(null);
103. 
104.                 // Set up the event listener BEFORE starting capture to not miss frames
105.                 const unlisten = await listen<BeaconFrame>('beacon-frame', (event) => {
106.                     const beacon = event.payload;
107. 
108.                     // Update deduplicated map (keyed by BSSID)
109.                     beaconsRef.current.set(beacon.bssid, beacon);
110. 
111.                     // Append to stream (ring buffer)
112.                     streamRef.current.push(beacon);
113.                     if (streamRef.current.length > maxStreamLength) {
114.                         streamRef.current = streamRef.current.slice(-maxStreamLength);
115.                     }
116. 
117.                     scheduleUpdate();
118.                 });
119. 
120.                 unlistenRef.current = unlisten;
121. 
122.                 // Start the capture on the Rust backend
123.                 const status = await invoke<CaptureStatus>('start_capture', {
124.                     interfaceName,
125.                 });
126. 
127.                 setIsCapturing(status.active);
128.             } catch (err: unknown) {
129.                 const message =
130.                     err instanceof Error
131.                         ? err.message
132.                         : typeof err === 'string'
133.                             ? err
134.                             : JSON.stringify(err);
135.                 setError(message);
136.                 setIsCapturing(false);
137. 
138.                 // Clean up the listener if capture failed to start
139.                 if (unlistenRef.current) {
140.                     unlistenRef.current();
141.                     unlistenRef.current = null;
142.                 }
143.             }
144.         },
145.         [maxStreamLength, scheduleUpdate]
146.     );
147. 
148.     const stopCapture = useCallback(async () => {
149.         try {
150.             await invoke<CaptureStatus>('stop_capture');
151.             setIsCapturing(false);
152.         } catch (err: unknown) {
153.             const message =
154.                 err instanceof Error
155.                     ? err.message
156.                     : typeof err === 'string'
157.                         ? err
158.                         : JSON.stringify(err);
159.             setError(message);
160.         } finally {
161.             // Always clean up the event listener
162.             if (unlistenRef.current) {
163.                 unlistenRef.current();
164.                 unlistenRef.current = null;
165.             }
166.             if (updateTimerRef.current) {
167.                 clearTimeout(updateTimerRef.current);
168.                 updateTimerRef.current = null;
169.             }
170.         }
171.     }, []);
172. 
173.     // Cleanup on unmount
174.     useEffect(() => {
175.         return () => {
176.             if (unlistenRef.current) {
177.                 unlistenRef.current();
178.             }
179.             if (updateTimerRef.current) {
180.                 clearTimeout(updateTimerRef.current);
181.             }
182.         };
183.     }, []);
184. 
185.     return {
186.         beacons,
187.         beaconStream,
188.         isCapturing,
189.         error,
190.         startCapture,
191.         stopCapture,
192.     };
193. }
194. 
195. // --- Context Provider to prevent state loss on tab switch ---
196. 
197. const BeaconCaptureContext = createContext<UseBeaconCaptureResult | null>(null);
198. 
199. export function BeaconCaptureProvider({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) {
200.     const captureState = useBeaconCaptureInternal();
201.     return (
202.         <BeaconCaptureContext.Provider value= { captureState } >
203.         { children }
204.         </BeaconCaptureContext.Provider>
205.     );
206. }
207. 
208. export function useBeaconCapture() {
209.     const context = useContext(BeaconCaptureContext);
210.     if (!context) {
211.         throw new Error("useBeaconCapture must be used within a BeaconCaptureProvider");
212.     }
213.     return context;
214. }
215.
````

</details>
